checklist_items.title: Enforce strong authentication for forum submissions to verify RFRV origin
checklist_items.description: Implement multi-factor authentication linking forum accounts to on-chain identities to prevent impersonation of the BGT Foundation during RFRV submissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain verification of governance proposal execution to prevent tampering
checklist_items.description: Ensure that each proposal execution is recorded on-chain with cryptographic proofs to prevent repudiation and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Actions on a public blockchain are inherently non-repudiable due to transaction history.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Maintain secure, immutable, and attributable audit trails of all RFRV review activities and final decisions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain transaction records showing proposal execution and approval signatures.

checklist_items.title: Validate whitelist status of Reward Vaults before directing $BGT emissions
checklist_items.description: Smart contract controlling $BGT emissions must verify that target Reward Vaults are whitelisted and active prior to transfer execution.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A validator might maliciously or accidentally direct emissions to non-functional or non-whitelisted vaults.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict on-chain verification that reward vaults are whitelisted and active before emissions are processed.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code audit logs indicating whitelist checks and transaction logs showing validation steps.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of validators and reward managers using multisig and hardware wallets
checklist_items.description: Employ multisignature schemes and hardware wallets for validator and reward manager keys to prevent unauthorized signing and Spoofing of emission or reward distribution transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: If a Validator's private key is compromised, an attacker can spoof emission direction transactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, multisig, and operational security best practices for private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security policies and hardware wallet audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction signing in hardware wallets and verify all transaction parameters before signing
checklist_items.description: Require Validators and reward managers to review transaction details, including emission targets and amounts, displayed explicitly on hardware wallets or trusted interfaces prior to signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users might sign transactions with altered parameters if not carefully verified.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require all transaction details to be explicitly shown and confirmed on hardware wallets prior to signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Device logs showing transaction review process.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strong cryptographic authentication for RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Implement cryptographic verification methods, such as digital signatures linked to verified on-chain identities, to authenticate submitters of RFRV proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing cryptographic verification of submitter identities.

checklist_items.title: Validate Reward Vault whitelist status before directing $BGT emissions
checklist_items.description: In the reward distribution process, verify that the target Reward Vault is whitelisted and active before sending emissions to prevent directing funds to malicious or unapproved contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system (e.g., Distributor Contract 214) might not robustly check the status/validity of a target Reward Vault before processing emissions, or this check could be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain verification that target Reward Vaults are whitelisted and active before emissions are distributed.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing whitelist checks during emission distribution.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of validators and governance signers using hardware security modules (HSMs) or multi-signature arrangements
checklist_items.description: Manage validator and governance private keys with hardware wallets, HSMs, or multi-signature setups to prevent unauthorized signing of emission directions or administrative actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Validator's private key can be compromised, allowing an attacker to spoof emission directives or governance actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement secure key storage with hardware wallets, HSMs, and require multi-party signatures for sensitive operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policy documentation and key access logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive input validation and transaction parameter verification in wallets and dApps
checklist_items.description: Ensure that user interfaces and wallets verify all transaction details, such as amounts, addresses, and parameters, prior to signing and broadcasting, to prevent tampering by malicious interfaces or malware.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The user's device or wallet interface may be compromised, altering transaction details prior to signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, verify transaction details on trusted screens, and educate users on best security practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User acceptance test reports showing verification steps, or wallet UI audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Enable HTTPS and certificate pinning for all RPC endpoints and wallet connections
checklist_items.description: Ensure all RPC and wallet communication channels between users and the blockchain are secured with TLS, and implement certificate pinning to prevent Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RPC communication may be intercepted and modified if not secured, and transport security is not consistently enforced.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS connections with strict certificate validation and implement certificate pinning in wallets.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network traffic captures demonstrating TLS enforcement and pinning effectiveness.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication for BGT Foundation's review decisions, such as digital signatures or multi-party consensus.
checklist_items.description: Given the risk of impersonation or repudiation of review decisions, enforce mechanisms that make decisions non-repudiable and verifiable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The process for recording and finalizing review decisions by the BGT Foundation does not create a tamper-proof, attributable record.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Maintain secure, immutable, and attributable audit trails of all RFRV review activities and final decisions, possibly using on-chain signatures or group signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs or signed decision records.

checklist_items.title: Enforce access controls and secure development practices for the Berachain NFT Bridge smart contract.
checklist_items.description: Prevent tampering or exploits in the bridge contracts that could lead to loss of assets or system corruption.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Berachain NFT Bridge contract (ID 211) contains a flaw in its logic for handling NFT data or state transitions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct security audits and formal verification of bridge contracts; implement secure development lifecycle practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification proof.

checklist_items.title: Verify that the Reward Vault and associated admin controls are secured with multi-signature and timelock mechanisms.
checklist_items.description: Prevent privilege escalation or unauthorized control over reward distribution and administrative functions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The administrative controls for the Reward Vault are vulnerable or overly permissive.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-signature governance for admin actions, timelocks, and conduct regular security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration audits, multisig transaction logs.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure private key management practices for validator keys involved in block proposal and reward signing.
checklist_items.description: Mitigate the risk of elevation of privilege and tampering with consensus-critical keys.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's block signing key or reward signing key can be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules (HSMs), hardware wallets, multi-signature approval, and enforce operational security best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audit reports, hardware wallet configurations.

checklist_items.title: Require explicit verification and user review of all transaction parameters in wallets prior to signing, especially for cross-chain and DeFi transactions.
checklist_items.description: Prevent tampering and client-side manipulation of transaction details, reducing the risk of fraud or loss.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User does not meticulously verify transaction details before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Design wallet interfaces to display all transaction parameters clearly; educate users about verifying transaction details.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of transaction reviews, user attestations.

checklist_items.title: Implement multiple, verifiable authentication factors for actions with significant security impact, such as governance proposals, token delegation, and reward claims.
checklist_items.description: Reduce risk of spoofing and impersonation by requiring multi-factor authentication or cryptographic proof.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Actions are susceptible to spoofing or impersonation due to weak authentication mechanisms.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy multi-factor authentication (e.g., wallet-based, threshold signatures, hardware security modules).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Authentication logs, MFA setup confirmation.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication mechanisms for the governance review process
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all RFRV submissions are verified through cryptographic signatures or on-chain identity linking to prevent impersonation or spoofing by malicious actors.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Establish and enforce access controls for proposal modification and voting
checklist_items.description: Implement role-based access control and multi-party approval processes within governance smart contracts to prevent unauthorized tampering or malicious proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Contracts may allow proposal modifications without strict control, or attacker's account could be used to tamper with proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use role-based access control and multi-signature approval for proposal modification and voting functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs or smart contract code verifying access restrictions.

checklist_items.title: Configure and monitor transaction signing in secure hardware wallets
checklist_items.description: Require validators and governance participants to sign critical transactions using hardware wallets with verified firmware to prevent tampering and privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's transaction signing environment (e.g., wallet, RPC connection) can be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require use of hardware wallets and verify transaction details before signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration documentation and transaction signatures from hardware wallets.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure development and audit practices for smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Require comprehensive security audits, formal verification, and use of vetted libraries for all smart contracts controlling protocol assets and critical functions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault smart contract deployed by a Protocol contains exploitable flaws.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct security audits, formal verification, and use of standard libraries.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and verification certificates.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain whitelisting and validation of Reward Vaults and incentive distributions
checklist_items.description: In smart contracts handling incentive distribution, verify that the target vaults are whitelisted and active before processing emissions or rewards to prevent diverted or malicious allocations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator can direct emissions to non-whitelisted Reward Vaults or faulty ones.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict on-chain validation of vault whitelisting and status.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Contract code and audit logs showing whitelisting checks.

checklist_items.title: Use multi-factor authentication (MFA) and robust key management for validator and admin keys
checklist_items.description: Secure validator keys and admin control keys with hardware wallets, multi-signature setups, and regular key rotation to prevent privilege escalation and unauthorized control.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's private key can be compromised, leading to privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Manage private keys with hardware wallets, multi-sig, and secure operational practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies and configuration documentation.

checklist_items.title: Verify strong authentication mechanisms for Guardian multisig signing.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the multisig process requiring 5 of 9 Guardian signatures is protected against key compromise through hardware security modules and multi-factor authentication.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can compromise at least 5 Guardian private keys.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-signature security practices with hardware wallets and multi-factor authentication.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of Guardian key access and multisig signature approvals.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive smart contract security audits for Reward Vaults before deployment.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that Reward Vault smart contracts undergo formal security audits to prevent vulnerabilities that could lead to fund loss or DoS conditions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts might contain vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct thorough security audits with reputable firms before deployment.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and security verification certificates.

checklist_items.title: Require on-chain proof of governance proposal creation to prevent repudiation.
checklist_items.description: Design governance proposal submission process so that each proposal is accompanied by an on-chain digital signature or timestamp that is publicly verifiable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance proposal submissions lack strong, verifiable attribution.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain signed proposals or immutable logs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain transactions or signed messages showing author attribution.

checklist_items.title: Deploy phishing-resistant wallet security features for users.
checklist_items.description: Encourage the use of hardware wallets and implement wallet UI features that verify transaction details before signing, to prevent tampering or phishing attacks during token transfers and interactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User interacts with a compromised wallet or malicious frontend.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Educate users, promote hardware wallet usage, validate transaction details in wallets.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Wallet configuration and user compliance logs.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access control checks in smart contracts for incentive management functions.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only authorized entities (e.g., designated Token Managers) can add incentives or set incentive rates, using role-based permissions verified via multisig or well-tested access control patterns.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault's functions may have flawed authorization logic.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement role-based access control with multisig and audit controls.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review and access control verification reports.

checklist_items.title: Verify and monitor oracle integrity for dynamic mint rate and basket mode decisions.
checklist_items.description: Use decentralized, multiple sources for oracles providing collateral prices and basket parameters, with on-chain validation and community oversight to prevent manipulation affecting minting or collateral backing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Oracle prices or basket conditions can be manipulated for profit or destabilization.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy decentralized oracle frameworks with on-chain consensus and regular audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Oracle aggregation audit logs and discrepancy reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement user education programs about verifying contract addresses before interactions.
checklist_items.description: Provide clear guidance and warnings to users to verify smart contract addresses, URLs, and transaction details in their wallets to prevent interaction with spoofed or malicious contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may not verify authenticity of interfaces or addresses.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Develop educational materials, alerts, and verification prompts in wallets.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training compliance metrics and wallet usage logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify that forum requires wallet-based login for RFRV submitters
checklist_items.description: Ensure the governance forum enforces wallet signature verification or on-chain identity links to authenticate submitters of RFRV proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum (223) does not have robust mechanisms to verify the true identity of RFRV submitters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., digital signatures, linking to verified on-chain identity)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing signature verification or identity link at submission.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain verification for governance proposal creation
checklist_items.description: Ensure proposals are only accepted if the submitter holds the required minimum $BGT stake, verified via smart contract checks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There is a vulnerability in the Governance Contracts (212) related to determining voting power or proposal creation requirements.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits and formal verification of proposal creation logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs with proposal creation data and validator signatures.

checklist_items.title: Use official contract addresses and domain validation for bridge and asset interactions
checklist_items.description: Ensure all user interactions with bridges and asset contracts are directed to verified, whitelisted addresses to prevent spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users cannot reliably verify the authenticity of the bridge interface or smart contract addresses.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Publish and regularly update an official list of contract addresses and domain names; educate users on verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Certificate transparency logs, official announcements, or audit reports confirming addresses.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for administrative controls on Reward Vaults and Governance contracts
checklist_items.description: Require MFA or multisignature approval for sensitive operations, such as updating incentive rates or administrative parameters.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for functions like adding incentives or setting rates is flawed or bypassable.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement role-based access controls with multisig and MFA for admin operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs with MFA attestations; multisig approval records.

checklist_items.title: Regularly conduct security audits and formal verification of bridge smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Audit the architecture and code of the bridge contracts to identify and fix vulnerabilities that could lead to minting unbacked tokens or asset theft.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The bridge's smart contracts or its infrastructure have an exploitable vulnerability.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Commission audits from reputable security firms; implement formal verification techniques.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure key management with hardware security modules (HSMs) and multisig for validator keys
checklist_items.description: Protect validator private keys used for block proposal, signing, and imission from compromise through hardware-based key storage and multisignature approvals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's block signing key can be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules and multisig for key operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key custody policies, audit logs of key access, hardware module validation reports.

checklist_items.title: Enforce cryptographic signatures to verify the identity of forum submitters for RFRV proposals
checklist_items.description: Ensure that every RFRV submission is signed with the submitter's verified on-chain identity to prevent impersonation (Threat ID 1).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed message or cryptographic proof linking forum account to on-chain identity.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain verification of token holdings for proposal creation
checklist_items.description: Require that the smart contract verifying RFRV proposals checks the submitter’s on-chain token balance or delegation status to meet proposal thresholds (Threat ID 75).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Contracts (212) do not automatically verify the proposer's $BGT balance/delegation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Embed on-chain balance/delegation checks within the proposal creation process.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing automatic balance check during proposal submission.

checklist_items.title: Require secure private key management and multi-factor authentication for voting and staking operations
checklist_items.description: Ensure users and validators use hardware wallets or multi-signature setups to prevent private key compromise (Threat IDs 2, 3, 4, 7).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Attacker gains access to private keys of users or validators to perform spoofing or privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Adopt hardware wallets, enforce multi-signature requirements, conduct regular key management audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit records of key management procedures, hardware wallet usage logs.

checklist_items.title: Perform comprehensive security audits and formal verification of smart contracts involved in reward distribution, vault management, and bridge operations
checklist_items.description: Mitigate coding vulnerabilities that could be exploited for tampering, DoS, or privilege escalation (Threat IDs 24, 26, 27).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward vaults, bridges, and incentive contracts may contain vulnerabilities or flawed logic.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable security auditors, use formal verification tools, and follow secure development best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, code review documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure authentication mechanisms and cryptographic signatures for proposal, voting, and redemption processes
checklist_items.description: Each critical operation should be authenticated via signed messages linked to verified on-chain identities to prevent spoofing and repudiation (Threat IDs 16, 22, 27).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak authentication mechanisms can be exploited for spoofing or repudiation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Mandate cryptographic signatures from verified on-chain accounts for all proposal submissions, votes, and redemptions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed transaction logs, cryptographic proof of identity.

checklist_items.title: Provide user education and warnings about interacting only with official, verified interfaces and URLs for all governance, staking, and transaction activities
checklist_items.description: Reduce social engineering risk and prevent users from falling victim to spoofed or phishing sites (Threat IDs 22, 27, 52, 53, 54).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may be deceived by malicious interfaces or links.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Distribute clear guidelines, official URLs, and awareness campaigns.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User feedback, security awareness materials, incident reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication mechanisms for RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Governance Forum employs robust identity verification, such as linking submissions to verified on-chain identities or cryptographic signatures, to prevent impersonation or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing linked on-chain identities at the time of submission.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain verification of proposal creators and reviewers
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all proposals and reviews on the Governance Forum are authenticated through on-chain signatures or verified identities to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system relies on off-chain identities without on-chain verification, allowing spoofing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Integrate on-chain signature verification for proposal submissions and reviews.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit reports confirming implementation of signature verification.

checklist_items.title: Validate reward vault whitelisting before emission distribution
checklist_items.description: Implement on-chain checks to ensure that reward vaults are whitelisted and active before directing $BGT emissions, preventing funds from being sent to malicious or unverified vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator can direct emissions to unverified or malicious vaults due to lack of on-chain validation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Add on-chain whitelist verification in the emission distribution process.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs and smart contract code demonstrating whitelist verification logic.

checklist_items.title: Secure validator private keys with hardware security modules (HSMs) and multi-signature signatures
checklist_items.description: Ensure all validator keys used for signing emission directives or proposal approvals are stored securely using HSMs and require multiple signatures for critical actions, preventing single-point key compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are stored on insecure devices or single signatures can authorize critical actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Store keys in HSMs and enforce multi-signature approval workflows.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policy documentation and audit logs showing multi-signature transaction authorizations.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure, verified RPC endpoints with HTTPS and DNSSEC validation
checklist_items.description: Configure wallet and dApp connections to use only verified RPC endpoints secured with HTTPS and validated DNSSEC to prevent MitM and DNS spoofing attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users connect to malicious or compromised RPC endpoints without verification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Provide official RPC endpoint list with HTTPS and DNSSEC validation; educate users.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration documentation and implementation checklist.

checklist_items.title: Implement account access controls with multi-factor authentication (MFA) for BGT Foundation members
checklist_items.description: Require MFA for all BGT Foundation account accesses and governance interfaces to prevent privilege escalation from account compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak access controls in foundation accounts allow privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce MFA and regular access audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs showing MFA enforcement and audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Configure proper transaction confirmation in wallets to avoid tampering
checklist_items.description: Advise users to enable transaction confirmation prompts in wallets to review all parameters (amounts, addresses, slippage) before signing, reducing tampering risk.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users do not verify transaction details before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: User education and wallet configuration best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training materials and wallet configuration documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication mechanisms for RFRV submissions (e.g., wallet-based login, digital signatures)
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Governance Forum employs robust authentication, such as linking submissions to verified on-chain identities, to prevent spoofing or impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum (223) lacks strong mechanisms to verify the true identity of RFRV submitters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., digital signatures, linking to verified on-chain identities)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing authenticated identities associated with RFRV submissions.

checklist_items.title: Configure blockchain smart contracts with rigorous access controls and role-based permissions for proposal and vote management
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the governance smart contracts (e.g., 212) employ role-based access control with least privileges, and regularly audit their logic to prevent elevation of privilege and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance contracts may have flaws in access control logic, allowing an attacker to bypass restrictions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and audit role-based access control and least privilege principles
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control audit reports, code review documentation.

checklist_items.title: Enable immutable logging for all governance proposal and decision events
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the on-chain governance proposal, voting, and decision events are stored with cryptographically verifiable, immutable logs to prevent repudiation and support accountability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance process lacks non-repudiable evidence linking actions to specific entities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement comprehensive on-chain logging with cryptographic signatures where applicable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain event logs, audit trail documentation.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-signature approval for critical governance actions and proposal cancellations
checklist_items.description: Implement multi-signature or quorum-based approval mechanisms for governance proposal cancellations to prevent tampering and ensure consensus.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A flaw in the multisig verification could allow a single compromised key to authorize cancellations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Design and enforce robust multisig protocols with formal verification and regular audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig transaction records, security audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure key management and multi-factor authentication for governance guardians and administrators
checklist_items.description: Ensure that private keys controlling governance actions are stored securely using hardware security modules (HSMs) or multi-party multi-signature schemes, coupled with multi-factor authentication.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Guardians' private keys may be compromised if not properly secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, secure key storage, and multi-party signature schemes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies, key storage audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Use secure and verified communication channels for off-chain coordination among Guardians
checklist_items.description: Establish and enforce secure, encrypted communication protocols (e.g., end-to-end encrypted messaging, verified channels) for Guardians collaborating on sensitive governance decisions to prevent information disclosure and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Guardians' off-chain coordination may be leaked or tampered with if not secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Employ secure messaging platforms, enforce operational security policies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Communication protocol configurations, operational security policies.

checklist_items.title: Ensure only whitelisted and verified Reward Vaults are eligible for $BGT emissions
checklist_items.description: Maintain a trusted and transparent whitelist of Reward Vault contracts and verify their status before directing emissions, to prevent diversion to malicious or unauthorized vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validators or emission controllers can direct $BGT to unverified or malicious vaults if whitelisting is not enforced.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain whitelist checks and status verification for Reward Vaults before emission transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Whitelist registry audits, transaction logs showing enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Verify strong authentication mechanisms for governance proposal submissions
checklist_items.description: Check if the governance proposal process enforces the use of wallet-based authentication or multi-factor authentication to prevent spoofed or malicious proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance system may lack strong, cryptographically verifiable attribution for proposal submissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement blockchain-based digital signatures or multi-party approval for proposal submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing proposal submission signatures and verification procedures.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain verification for Reward Vault whitelisting status
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the Reward Vault whitelisting status is checked on-chain before sending $BGT emissions to prevent directing funds to non-whitelisted or malicious vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system might not robustly check the status/validity of a target Reward Vault before emissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Add on-chain whitelist verification in emission distribution transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit reports and transaction logs showing whitelist checks.

checklist_items.title: Secure validator private keys and enforce multi-signature controls for reward emission directives
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the validator's private keys used for signing $BGT emission transactions are stored securely using hardware security modules or multi-signature schemes to prevent spoofing or unauthorized directives.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Validator's private key used for signing emission directives can be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, multisig, and operational security best practices for key storage.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policy documentation and security audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Employ secure coding practices and conduct regular security audits of Reward Vault smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Periodically review and audit Reward Vault contracts to identify and fix vulnerabilities related to reentrancy, integer overflows, or access control flaws that could be exploited for tampering or DoS.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts might contain exploitable vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Security audits, formal verification, and best practices for smart contract development.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, and audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement anti-phishing measures and verify contract addresses before user interactions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that users are guided to verify the official contract addresses and use trusted interfaces when interacting with bridge or reward contracts to prevent spoofing and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users cannot reliably distinguish fake contract interfaces from legitimate ones.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use official links, verify contract addresses, wallet warnings, and educate users.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training materials, interface design reviews, phishing awareness reports.
checklist_items.owner: User Experience and Security Awareness Team

checklist_items.title: Enforce rate limiting and DDoS protection on critical infrastructure endpoints
checklist_items.description: Protect RPC endpoints, governance portals, and contract interaction interfaces against high volume attacks to prevent DoS and ensure availability for legitimate users.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Endpoints are vulnerable to resource exhaustion and lack sufficient DoS mitigation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rate limiting, Shield services, and infrastructure redundancy.
checklist_items.evidence_required: System performance logs, security incident reports.

checklist_items.title: Require hardware security modules or multi-signature schemes for critical operations
checklist_items.description: Ensure that any administrative actions or sensitive operations in smart contracts are authorized via multi-signature validation or HSMs to prevent elevation of privilege through key compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Administrative controls may be weak or keys could be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce multi-signature approval workflows and use hardware security modules.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control policy documents, multi-sig transaction logs.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strong multi-factor authentication for governance proposal creators
checklist_items.description: Implement MFA for all users submitting proposals to prevent impersonation or unauthorized submissions, especially those with high voting power.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance system may lack sufficient verification of proposal creators' identities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-factor authentication and verify proposal submission identities
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing MFA successful login before proposal submission.

checklist_items.title: Require cryptographic signatures for proposals and votes with verifiable on-chain attribution
checklist_items.description: Use cryptographic signatures linked to verified identities to attribute proposals and votes, preventing repudiation or denial of actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Lack of cryptographic proof of proposal or vote origin.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain signatures and audit trail mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample proposal/vote submissions with cryptographic signatures verified on-chain.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure, verified RPC endpoints for wallet connections
checklist_items.description: Configure wallet software and dApps to connect only through verified, HTTPS-enforced RPC endpoints to prevent MitM attacks and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User relies on unverified or insecure RPC endpoints, risking data tampering and Privacy leaks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Using authenticated, encrypted RPC connections and educating users to connect only to official endpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration verification reports or protocol documentation confirming secure RPC setup.

checklist_items.title: Restrict token listing to verified, vetted contracts only
checklist_items.description: Establish governance or approval mechanisms to approve only verified smart contracts for token listing on DEXs or bridges to prevent spoofed or malicious token contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Unverified token listings can be malicious or spoofed, leading to user loss.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement listing approval process with multi-party verification and strong community audit.
checklist_items.evidence_required: List of approved tokens with audit reports and verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify contract addresses before interaction in user wallets
checklist_items.description: Educate users and integrate wallet features to verify and display the actual deployed contract address of tokens or dApps before signing transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users often do not verify the contract addresses or URLs, leading to interactions with malicious contracts or sites.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: User education campaigns and wallet warnings for unverified addresses.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots or logs showing user verified contract addresses before transaction signing.

checklist_items.title: Deploy smart contracts with security best practices, including formal audits and bug bounty programs
checklist_items.description: Ensure all smart contracts, especially those managing assets like Reward Vaults and Bridges, undergo rigorous security audits and peer reviews before deployment.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Contract vulnerabilities can lead to exploits, fund loss, and system downtime.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement formal verification, audits, and continuous bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, bug bounty submissions.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-party approval protocols for sensitive operations such as proposal cancellations or emission direction changes
checklist_items.description: Use multi-signature or multi-party consent mechanisms for critical governance or asset management transactions to prevent unilateral malicious actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Single-party actions without multi-party approval are vulnerable to malicious or accidental misuse.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multisig approval for all critical governance or treasury operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Approval logs showing multi-party consent for such transactions.

checklist_items.title: Ensure off-chain proposal review and approval processes are comprehensively logged and time-stamped with multi-party signatures
checklist_items.description: Implement immutable audit logs and digital signatures for all off-chain proposal reviews and decisions to prevent repudiation or dispute.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Internal review decisions are susceptible to dispute without proper logging and signatures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use blockchain-based or cryptographically signed audit logs for reviews.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed audit trail records with timestamps and signatures.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strong cryptographic authentication for forum submitters of RFRVs
checklist_items.description: Check if the platform requires users to authenticate via wallet signatures or verified on-chain identities before submitting RFRVs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs or system documentation verifying the authentication process for forum submissions.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain verification and attestation for governance proposal execution
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all governance proposal actions are recorded on-chain with cryptographic proof linking the proposal to the responsible entity, enabling non-repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The process for recording and finalizing review decisions by the BGT Foundation does not create a tamper-proof, attributable record of who made the decision and when.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Maintain secure, immutable, and attributable audit trails of all RFRV review activities and final decisions made by the BGT Foundation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain transaction records or signed audit logs demonstrating proposal decisions.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of validators and governance officials using hardware wallets and multi-signature controls
checklist_items.description: Verify that key management practices for validator and admin keys include hardware security modules and multisig setups to prevent unauthorized signing or control.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The validator's environment or tools are compromised, allowing an attacker to sign malicious transactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure hardware wallets, enforce multi-factor authentication, rotate keys regularly, and conduct security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management system audit reports and wallet setup documentation.

checklist_items.title: Configure smart contracts with explicit whitelists and access controls for token emissions and administration functions
checklist_items.description: Review and verify that all reward and incentive smart contracts implement strict role-based access control and whitelist validation to prevent unauthorized modifications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract may not properly authenticate that the caller is the designated Token Manager, or the Token Manager's private key is compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Ensure strong authentication (e.g., role-based access control, whitelists) in the Reward Vault contract for all sensitive functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports demonstrating access control mechanisms and whitelist configuration.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure deployment and auditing processes for Reward Vault contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure all Reward Vault smart contracts are deployed following best practices, undergo formal security audits, and include upgrade or emergency pause mechanisms with proper governance controls.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault smart contracts could contain bugs or malicious logic exploitable by attackers.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct thorough security audits, formal verification, and implement upgrade pathways governed by multisig or DAO.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit certifications, formal verification reports, and deployment records.

checklist_items.title: Enforce domain and contract address verification for dApp interactions and token operations
checklist_items.description: Verify that users are only directed to official URLs and that contract addresses are checked against known, approved lists before performing any critical operations such as swaps, minting, or staking.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User cannot reliably distinguish a fake interface or contract from the legitimate one.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use trusted bookmarks, verify contracts via official channels, and educate users on safe practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of verified URLs or logs of address verification steps.
checklist_items.owner: Community Outreach and Platform Security Team

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting and anti-spam measures on governance and RFRV submission platforms
checklist_items.description: Configure platforms to limit the number of proposals or RFRV submissions per user or IP within a given timeframe to prevent spam and denial-of-service attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance system may be vulnerable to spam proposals due to insufficient filtering or high proposal thresholds.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy CAPTCHAs, IP filtering, and proposal submission rate limits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Platform logs showing submission rates over time.

checklist_items.title: Ensure all smart contract and protocol implementations undergo formal security audits and utilize standard libraries
checklist_items.description: Conduct manual and automated security reviews of all deployed smart contracts, enforce use of well-vetted libraries, and participate in bug bounty programs to discover潜在漏洞。
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may contain vulnerabilities if not properly audited.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct formal verifications, code audits, and maintain a bug bounty program.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, verification certificates, and bug bounty results.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication mechanisms for RFRV submissions, such as linking forum accounts to on-chain identities or digital signatures.
checklist_items.description: To prevent spoofing of RFRV submissions, verify that only authenticated and verified users can submit proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing verified user identities at submission time.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain verification of Reward Vault whitelisting status before processing emissions to prevent directing funds to unverified or malicious vaults.
checklist_items.description: To mitigate the risk of directing incentives to malicious or unwhitelisted vaults, verify their status on-chain during emission distribution.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system may not robustly check the status/validity of Reward Vaults before emitting funds, or this check could be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict on-chain verification that target Reward Vaults are whitelisted and active before emissions are processed.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain transaction logs confirming the check and emission to a whitelisted vault.

checklist_items.title: Require validator key management best practices, including hardware security modules (HSMs), multi-signature setups, and operational security, to prevent key compromise leading to emission spoofing.
checklist_items.description: To prevent spoofing via validator key compromise, ensure secure key storage and management procedures.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator's private key used for signing emission direction transactions can be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure validator key management (e.g., HSMs, robust operational security). Optional: Implement a cooldown period or multi-signature requirement for changing emission directions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of key access and management procedures.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure development, regular security audits, and formal verification for all smart contracts, especially Reward Vaults and bridge contracts, to eliminate vulnerabilities exploited for tampering and DoS.
checklist_items.description: To mitigate the risk of contract vulnerabilities leading to tampering or DoS, ensure thorough testing and formal verification processes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault smart contracts deployed by the Protocol (Actor 5) might contain exploitable flaws.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits, formal verification, use of secure libraries, and bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and verification certificates.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-party, cryptographically verified on-chain approval for significant governance actions to prevent repudiation and unauthorized tampering.
checklist_items.description: To prevent repudiation and tampering with governance actions, ensure on-chain approval mechanisms are cryptographically attributable and auditable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The process for recording and finalizing review decisions by the BGT Foundation does not create a tamper-proof, attributable record.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Maintain secure, immutable, and attributable audit trails of all governance review activities and decisions, such as group digital signatures or on-chain records.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain logs or cryptographically signed review records.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure, verified communication channels for Guardians and the BGT Foundation, including multi-factor authentication and confidential off-chain communication mechanisms, to prevent leakage of sensitive governance review information.
checklist_items.description: To mitigate the risk of informational leakage leading to targeted attacks or governance manipulation, enforce secure communication practices among Guardians and Foundation members.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Guardians coordinate or discuss potential cancellations off-chain before executing the multisig transaction.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish secure and confidential communication channels for Guardian coordination, including encrypted messaging, secure channels, and operational security best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Communication logs or policy documentation of operational procedures.

checklist_items.title: Require all internal governance and protocol actions, such as proposal creation, voting, and execution, to be logged with strong cryptographic signatures and stored in tamper-evident on-chain records.
checklist_items.description: To mitigate repudiation and ensure accountability, record all governance activities with cryptographic signatures and on-chain auditable logs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The process for recording and finalizing review decisions by the BGT Foundation does not create a tamper-proof, attributable record.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Maintain secure, immutable, and attributable audit trails of all governance review activities and decisions, such as cryptographic signatures, multi-party signatures, or on-chain records.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain audit logs, signed activity records.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong on-chain identity verification for RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all participants submitting RFRVs are verified through cryptographic signatures or on-chain identity linkage to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Encrypt and restrict access to sensitive on-chain contributor data
checklist_items.description: Minimize on-chain storage of personally identifiable or highly specific individual vesting details if confidentiality is critical, and secure off-chain databases storing this information.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The exact vesting terms for each individual Initial Core Contributor are considered confidential.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Minimize on-chain storage of detailed individual vesting data; secure off-chain repositories.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit report demonstrating encrypted storage and strict access controls.

checklist_items.title: Conduct security audits focusing on denial of service vectors in vesting contracts
checklist_items.description: Perform thorough security audits of vesting smart contracts to identify and mitigate potential DoS vulnerabilities such as reentrancy or gas griefing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The vesting smart contract could contain bugs or be subject to administrative controls that can be misused or exploited.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of vesting contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and formal verification results.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-signature and role-based access controls on reward management contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure that functions for adding incentive tokens, setting incentive rates, and administrative controls are protected by multi-signature requirements and strict role-based permissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for functions like adding incentives or setting rates is flawed or bypassable.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and rigorously audit robust access control mechanisms (e.g., role-based access, multi-signature).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract source code and audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Auditing smart contracts for upgradeability and admin controls
checklist_items.description: Audit all Reward Vault contracts for potential vulnerabilities in administrative functions, upgrade mechanisms, and access controls to prevent privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract deployed by a Protocol contains software vulnerabilities or insecure admin controls.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits, use of secure upgrade patterns, role separation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, secure upgrade documentation.

checklist_items.title: Use secure oracle and off-chain proof mechanisms for incentive distribution
checklist_items.description: Implement robust, decentralized oracles and cryptographic proof mechanisms for incentivized actions, with regular audits and multi-party attestations, to prevent tampering and fraudulent claims.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The off-chain backend service generating proofs for incentive claims can be compromised or has vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure the backend proof generation service, validate proofs on-chain, consider decentralized proof mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs, cryptographic proof validation reports.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure, authenticated communication protocols for RPC endpoints
checklist_items.description: Ensure all RPC endpoints used for wallet connection are served over HTTPS, require strong authentication, and implement TLS with certificate pinning to prevent MitM and spoofing attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RPC endpoint is a single point of failure for the user's current session.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS, implement TLS with certificate pinning, educate users to verify endpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration files, TLS certificates, user verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction verification with all critical parameters explicitly displayed
checklist_items.description: Ensure wallet interfaces display all transaction details (recipient address, amount, contract address, fees) and require user confirmation before signing to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User does not meticulously verify all transaction details in their wallet before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Design wallet UIs to clearly display all transaction parameters and require explicit user confirmation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: UI design documentation, user testing reports.

checklist_items.title: Maintain immutable, on-chain audit logs of governance proposals and votes
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all proposals, votes, and governance actions are recorded on-chain with cryptographic signatures or multi-party attestations to prevent repudiation and provide traceability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The process for recording and finalizing review decisions by the BGT Foundation does not create a tamper-proof, attributable record.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain logging with cryptographic signatures and multi-party attestations for all governance activities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain event logs, cryptographic signature verification reports.

checklist_items.title: Verify that forum submission processes enforce strong user authentication to prevent impersonation of the BGT Foundation or project teams.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all RFRV submissions are authenticated via cryptographic signatures or verified identities to prevent spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Systems lack robust mechanisms to verify the authenticity of RFRV submitters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong cryptographic verification (e.g., digital signatures linked to verified on-chain identities) for all forum submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing verified cryptographic signatures on submissions.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain verification for governance proposals to prevent unauthorized or malicious proposal creation.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that smart contracts enforce minimum token holdings and proper signing to authorize proposal creation, preventing threshold bypass.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Vulnerabilities in the proposal creation logic may allow bypass of token thresholds.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Audit and verify proposal creation functions enforce correct token thresholds and signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit reports confirming enforcement of proposal threshold checks.

checklist_items.title: Use secure wallet practices and enable multi-factor authentication for all stakeholders performing sensitive actions such as proposal submission or voting.
checklist_items.description: Prevent privilege escalation and account compromise that can lead to unauthorized proposal creation or voting.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User accounts or wallet keys may be compromised due to weak security practices.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-factor authentication and user education on private key security.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training completion records and wallet security audits.

checklist_items.title: Apply strict input validation and verification for transaction parameters before signing or submitting actions via wallets or interfaces.
checklist_items.description: Prevent tampering of transaction data, such as redeems, stakes, or swaps, by malicious actors or compromised interfaces.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Transaction parameters may be altered by compromised wallet UI or malicious scripts.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement client-side verification, display all details explicitly, and use hardware wallets.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of transaction details in hardware wallets or signed confirmation logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure, Teir1 best practices for RPC endpoints, including HTTPS, authentication, and integrity protections.
checklist_items.description: Mitigate Man-in-the-Middle attacks and data tampering in RPC communications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RPC endpoints may be targeted for MitM or data interception.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS, VPNs, and verify endpoint authenticity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration audits showing HTTPS enforcement and network logs demonstrating secure communication.

checklist_items.title: Maintain robust access control and role management on smart contracts implementing critical functions like vault management, incentive distribution, and governance.
checklist_items.description: Prevent unauthorized privilege escalation or malicious control over core contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Security gaps in contract access control allow privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and audit role-based access controls, multi-sig for admin functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and formal verification results confirming access control robustness.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-signature approval and timelocks for critical governance actions to mitigate spoilers, tampering, and privilege escalation.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that proposals, cancellations, and privileged changes need consensus or time-based delays.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance processes can be manipulated if insufficient safeguards are in place.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multisig approvals, timelocks, and multi-party review processes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Governance process documentation and multisig transaction logs.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure key management practices, including hardware wallets, multisignatures, and operational security procedures for all entities with admin and validator privileges.
checklist_items.description: Prevent privilege escalation, key theft, and unauthorized control over smart contracts and network operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Private keys of validators and admins are at risk of compromise due to weak security.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, multisig, secure key vaults, and regular security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audit logs and operational security policies.

checklist_items.title: Verify whitelisted and active status of Reward Vaults before directing emissions or rewards.
checklist_items.description: Prevent misallocation or denial of incentives due to directing emissions to non-whitelisted or inactive vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator or emission process can be manipulated to send rewards to invalid or malicious vaults.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain verification that rewards are only sent to whitelisted and active vaults.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs and smart contract code verifying vault whitelisting checks.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive monitoring, alerting, and incident response plans for governance proposals, proposals broadcasting, and network operations.
checklist_items.description: Detect, prevent, and respond to malicious proposal creation, tampering, or spam.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: High proposal volume or malicious actors can overwhelm governance.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Set proposal creation thresholds, monitor proposal activity, and establish review procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Proposal submission logs, monitoring system alerts.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication linking forum accounts to verified on-chain identities for RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only users with verified on-chain identities can submit RFRVs to prevent impersonation or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Mandate that all governance proposals be signed by a verifiable digital signature linked to a trusted on-chain identity
checklist_items.description: Require proposals to include a cryptographic signature from a recognized on-chain identity to prevent repudiation and ensure non-repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak attribution mechanisms in governance proposals allow actors to deny their submissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce on-chain signatures from verified identities for all proposals
checklist_items.evidence_required: Presence of signature verification on submitted proposals.

checklist_items.title: Secure RPC endpoints using HTTPS and implement TLS to prevent Man-in-the-Middle attacks
checklist_items.description: Ensure all RPC communications are encrypted with TLS to protect against tampering and eavesdropping.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RPC endpoints may be vulnerable to MitM attacks if not properly secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS for all RPC communication channels.
checklist_items.evidence_required: TLS certificates and network logs showing encrypted connections.

checklist_items.title: Implement access controls with least privilege and multi-signature requirements for critical admin functions in Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Restrict admin operations on Reward Vault contracts to a multi-signature process and enforce least privilege to prevent elevation of privilege attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault admin controls may be vulnerable if not properly secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multi-signature wallets and strict role-based access controls for contract administration.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of admin actions and multisig signatures.

checklist_items.title: Maintain comprehensive audit logs with digital signatures for all governance proposal and decision activities
checklist_items.description: Ensure all governance activities are recorded on-chain with cryptographic signatures to establish non-repudiation and accountability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance actions may be denied or disputed without proper audit trails.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain signed logs of all proposals and votes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain records with signatures confirming proposal and vote origin.

checklist_items.title: Employ strict input validation and signed transaction verification in token minting and redemption contracts
checklist_items.description: Validate all inputs and signatures to prevent tampering and spoofing during minting and redemption processes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Minting and redemption processes may be vulnerable to tampering if signatures or inputs are insufficiently validated.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain verification of signatures and parameter checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports showing on-chain verification procedures.

checklist_items.title: Verify contract addresses and implement whitelisting to prevent interaction with malicious or spoofed Reward Vault contracts
checklist_items.description: Only allow interactions with verified and whitelisted Reward Vault addresses to prevent funds from being directed to malicious contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may interact with unverified or malicious contract addresses.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Maintain and enforce a whitelist of verified Reward Vaults.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Whitelist configuration and logs of interactions.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure private key management practices with hardware wallets and multi-signature controls for validators and key custodians
checklist_items.description: Prevent private key compromise to avoid elevation of privilege and spoofing in validator operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator keys are vulnerable if not protected using secure hardware modules.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require hardware security modules (HSMs), multi-signature, and operational security protocols for key storage.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management procedures and hardware deployment logs.

checklist_items.title: Enforce HTTPS and certificate pinning in all dApp and API interfaces to prevent impersonation and hijacking
checklist_items.description: Ensure all web interactions are secured with HTTPS and implement certificate pinning to prevent spoofing and MiTM attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may connect to malicious or compromised endpoints.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS and implement strict certificate validation/pinning.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Deployment configurations and TLS audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement user education programs emphasizing verification of contract addresses, website authenticity, and transaction details before signing
checklist_items.description: Educate users to verify the legitimacy of dApps, contract addresses, and transaction details to prevent social engineering and spoofing attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users are vulnerable to phishing and social engineering during interactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Develop and disseminate best practices, tutorials, and warning prompts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training records and simulated phishing test results.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication mechanisms for RFRV submissions, such as linking forum accounts to verified on-chain identities.
checklist_items.description: Verify if the Governance Forum requires users to authenticate via their wallet to submit RFRVs, ensuring identity verification.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum (223) does not have robust verification of submitter identities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require wallet-based login or verifiable off-chain identity proofs for RFRV submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing linkage of forum accounts to verified on-chain identities.

checklist_items.title: Enforce rigorous validation of transaction parameters before signing in wallets or dApps to prevent tampering during token swaps, staking, or redemption.
checklist_items.description: Verify if wallet interfaces display transaction details clearly and require user to review all parameters before signing, reducing risk of tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User does not verify transaction details in their wallet before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement wallet prompts that clearly display transaction details and require explicit user approval.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of wallet prompts showing transaction details before signing.

checklist_items.title: Use multi-factor authentication (MFA) for administrative access to Reward Vaults and governance contracts to prevent privilege escalation.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that access to critical administrative functions is protected by MFA, reducing the risk of unauthorized privilege escalation or administrative control takeover.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vaults and governance controls rely on private keys or accounts that could be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-signature wallets or hardware security modules for admin keys, enforce MFA for access portals.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing MFA enforcement on admin access.

checklist_items.title: Audit and verify the whitelisting process for Reward Vaults to ensure only legitimate, verified vaults are whitelisted for emissions.
checklist_items.description: Review the whitelisting procedures and their implementation to prevent malicious or spoofed Reward Vaults from being whitelisted.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system relies on the integrity of the whitelisting process to prevent malicious vaults from receiving emissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain verification of vault whitelisting status and multi-party approval for whitelisting decisions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs and blockchain records showing whitelisted vaults and approval process.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of validators and administrators used for signing emission directives and administrative actions, such as setting incentive rates.
checklist_items.description: Verify that key management practices include hardware wallets, multi-signature controls, and secure operational procedures to prevent key compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys can be compromised, leading to unauthorized emission control.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware secure modules, enforce operational security procedures, and limit the scope of key access.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audit reports, hardware wallet deployment records.

checklist_items.title: Verify the use of strong authentication mechanisms for forum submissions of RFRV proposals
checklist_items.description: Check if the governance forum requires users to authenticate via verified on-chain identities or multi-factor authentication before submitting RFRV proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing authenticated identity verification during RFRV submission.
