checklist_items.title: Ensure all on-chain reward distributions are governed by immutable and auditable smart contract logic
checklist_items.description: Audit the smart contracts responsible for reward distribution (e.g., Reward Vaults, Distributor) to confirm they enforce strict and tamper-proof logic for incentive allocation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contracts may contain vulnerabilities that allow unauthorized modification of incentive parameters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct thorough security audits of reward distribution smart contracts and formal verification where applicable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports or formal verification results demonstrating correct incentive logic.

checklist_items.title: Implement domain-based and TLS encryption for all RPC endpoints to prevent MitM tampering and data leakage
checklist_items.description: Verify that RPC endpoints used by users and validators employ HTTPS and are operated within trusted, verified domains. Consider enabling end-to-end encryption and using reputable RPC providers.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RPC endpoint is a single point of failure susceptible to MitM and tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS, verify domain authenticity, and use trusted providers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration audit documenting TLS setup and domain verification.

checklist_items.title: Use hardware security modules or multi-signature controls for private keys of validators and guardians
checklist_items.description: Ensure that private keys used for signing critical transactions (e.g., proposal cancellations, reward emissions) are stored in secure hardware wallets or protected via multi-signature schemes to prevent unauthorized elevation or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Privileged keys used for critical operations might be compromised or improperly secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement hardware security modules, enforce multi-sig, and conduct operational security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs, key management policies, and hardware wallet audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Establish a governance process with multi-party approval for critical parameters changes (e.g., reward rates, emission directions)
checklist_items.description: Implement governance procedures requiring multiple independent sign-offs or proposals before executing significant changes, reducing the risk of elevation of privilege through single-point compromises.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Unsecured or improperly controlled administrative functions could be exploited to bypass proper authorization.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require multi-party approvals, blockchain-based voting, or multisig governance for parameter updates.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Governance process documentation, multisig approval logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong on-chain identity verification for forum submissions
checklist_items.description: Check if the Governance Forum employs wallet-based authentication or other mechanisms to verify the identity of users submitting RFRVs, ensuring they are verified via on-chain identity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.
checklist_items.notes: Prioritize establishing verified identity links to prevent spoofed submissions.

checklist_items.title: Secure the communication channel between user and RPC endpoint using TLS
checklist_items.description: Verify that all RPC communications are secured with TLS to prevent Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) tampering of transaction data.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RPC communication is not consistently or effectively using transport layer security (e.g. HTTPS).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS for all RPC communications.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration audit showing TLS enabled for RPC endpoint.
checklist_items.notes: Ensure all users connect through verified secure endpoints.

checklist_items.title: Maintain an off-chain and on-chain audit trail of governance proposals and votes
checklist_items.description: Implement a mechanism to record all proposals and votes with cryptographic signatures or on-chain data to prevent repudiation and facilitate accountability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The process for recording and finalizing review decisions by the BGT Foundation does not create a tamper-proof, attributable record of who made the decision and when.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Maintain secure, immutable, and attributable audit trails of all review activities and final decisions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain transaction logs or signed audit reports.
checklist_items.notes: Regular audits and cryptographic signatures enhance accountability.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-signature and role-based access controls for reward distribution contracts
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Reward Vault and related contracts use multi-signature controls and strict role-based permissions for administrative functions to prevent privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for functions like adding incentives or setting rates is flawed or bypassable.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and rigorously audit robust role-based access control mechanisms, such as multisignatures or timelocks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit reports demonstrating role-based permissions.
checklist_items.notes: Adopt best practices for access management in smart contracts.

checklist_items.title: Use verified and audited token contracts for vault whitelisting
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all Reward Vaults listed in the whitelist have undergone security audits and are verified as legitimate before assigning emissions or rewards.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability in the Reward Vault's code could allow unauthorized modification or malicious deployment.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require all Reward Vaults to be audited and verified prior to whitelisting.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and whitelisted contract addresses from trusted sources.
checklist_items.notes: Implement strict onboarding procedures for Reward Vaults.

checklist_items.title: Enforce transaction parameter verification in client interfaces and smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Verify that all critical transaction parameters (e.g., amounts, addresses) are explicitly displayed and verified by users before signing, and validated on-chain where applicable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User does not meticulously verify all transaction details before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement detailed transaction review in wallets and smart contracts with sanity checks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Wallet transaction screenshots, smart contract audit reports.
checklist_items.notes: User education combined with technical safeguards reduces tampering risk.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust identity verification for forum submissions
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Governance Forum requires users to authenticate using an on-chain identity or verified credentials before submitting RFRV proposals to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum (Component 223) lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, digital signatures)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing verified identity linkage during submission.

checklist_items.title: Enforce whitelisting and validation of Reward Vaults during emission direction
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the Reward Vault responsible for receiving $BGT emissions is validated against a secure, on-chain whitelist before distributions are processed, to prevent directing funds to malicious or untrusted vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system (e.g., Distributor Contract 214) might not robustly check the whitelist status of Reward Vaults before emitting funds.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain verification of whitelist status before processing emissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing whitelist verification during each emission.

checklist_items.title: Secure private key management for Validator signers
checklist_items.description: Require Validators to store signing keys in hardware security modules (HSMs) or multisig setups and perform regular key audits, to prevent private key compromise that could lead to unauthorized emission direction or stake manipulation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator's private keys used for signing emission directives can be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, HSMs, and operate under strict operational procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports of key storage solutions and operational security practices.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication for Validator operations
checklist_items.description: Require multi-factor authentication before signing critical transactions like emission directives, stake, or validator registration, to prevent unauthorized control even if private keys are compromised.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator signing environments may be compromised or lack MFA safeguards.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce MFA in signing workflows.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of MFA enforcement during transaction signing.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure and verified RPC endpoints for user interactions
checklist_items.description: Use only official, verified RPC URLs with transport layer security (HTTPS) for all front-end interactions to prevent client-side man-in-the-middle or spoofing attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The User (Actor 1) relies on RPC endpoints which may be malicious or compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use only trusted RPC providers, enforce HTTPS, and warn users about untrusted endpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration audits and user wallet logs confirming use of official RPC URLs.

checklist_items.title: Enforce verification of transaction parameters before signing
checklist_items.description: Implement client-side or wallet-enforced checks to verify transaction details, including token amounts, addresses, and parameters, ensuring users review and confirm critical data before approval.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The User (Actor 1) does not verify transaction details thoroughly before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Integrate detailed transaction review in wallets with multi-sig or hardware wallet support.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User reports, wallet verification logs, or interface screenshots.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong user education on phishing and scam detection
checklist_items.description: Educate users (Actor 1) to only access official interfaces via bookmarks, verify domain names, and scrutinize transaction details to prevent falling for spoofing or phishing attacks on DEXs, bridges, and claim portals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users cannot reliably distinguish fake interfaces from legitimate ones.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct ongoing user awareness campaigns and provide official guidance.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User engagement metrics, training materials, phishing susceptibility reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong cryptographic authentication for Initial Core Contributors claiming vested tokens
checklist_items.description: Enforce cryptographic signatures or hardware wallet confirmation during token claim processes to prevent impersonation if private keys are compromised.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The authentication mechanism for claiming vested tokens relies on private key security, which can be weak or compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce strong authentication (e.g., cryptographic signatures) and educate contributors on private key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed claim transactions, audit logs of claim processes.

checklist_items.title: Minimize on-chain storage of detailed vesting information for confidentiality
checklist_items.description: Use aggregated data or merkle trees for vesting schedule details to prevent unintentional disclosure of sensitive contributor information.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The on-chain storage of individual vesting details can lead to unintended information disclosure.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Avoid storing detailed vesting information directly on-chain; use privacy-preserving techniques.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Design documents, code reviews showing use of aggregated data or merkle trees.

checklist_items.title: Conduct comprehensive security audits on the vesting and claim smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Audit the smart contracts responsible for vesting and token claiming to identify and fix vulnerabilities that could allow spoofing, tampering, or denial of service.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Vulnerabilities in smart contracts can be exploited to impersonate users, alter vesting data, or deny claims.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regular third-party security audits, formal verification, and continuous monitoring.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-signature or timelock controls for administrative functions of reward vaults and contracts
checklist_items.description: Control admin actions such as changing incentives, pausing contracts, or updating parameters via multi-signature wallets and timelocks to prevent privilege escalation or malicious updates.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Administrative controls are susceptible to privilege escalation if keys are compromised or controls are weak.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multisig wallets, enforce timelocks, segregate duties.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control policy documents, multisig implementation audits.

checklist_items.title: Enforce role-based access control (RBAC) in Reward Vault and governance contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure only authorized roles (e.g., designated Token Manager or Guardian) can perform sensitive functions by implementing strict RBAC mechanisms.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Access control flaws allow unauthorized role escalation or privilege misuse.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement fixed role assignments with strict on-chain access checks; regularly audit roles.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract access control code, role assignment logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify whitelisting and active status of Reward Vaults before directing incentives or tokens
checklist_items.description: Integrate on-chain checks to confirm Reward Vault is whitelisted, active, and compliant before executing incentive or token transfer transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Incentive transfers directed to non-whitelisted or inactive vaults lead to token loss or system malfunction.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: On-chain verification of vault whitelisting status prior to incentive distribution.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code, audit logs of incentive distributions.

checklist_items.title: Educate users on verifying transaction details and link URLs to prevent spoofing and phishing attacks
checklist_items.description: Enhance user awareness and wallet UI features to verify the authenticity of interfaces, contract addresses, and transaction details before signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may be deceived by fake interfaces, leading to asset loss.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: User education, official link verification, wallet warnings.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Training materials, wallet UI updates, user reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication for forum submissions of RFRV proposals.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only verified and authenticated entities can submit RFRV proposals to prevent spoofing or unauthorized submissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum (223) does not have robust mechanisms to verify the true identity of RFRV submitters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., digital signatures, verified accounts) for forum submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing verified identities attached to RFRV submissions.

checklist_items.title: Require on-chain identity verification for critical actions such as token claim and redemption.
checklist_items.description: Link user off-chain actions to on-chain identities to prevent spoofing and impersonation during token claiming and redeeming processes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The claim and redemption mechanisms rely solely on private key signatures, which, if compromised, enable spoofing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use linked on-chain identity verification methods alongside cryptographic signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Verification records linking off-chain actions to on-chain identities.

checklist_items.title: Ensure smart contracts used in Reward Vaults are subject to thorough security audits and formal verification.
checklist_items.description: Conduct extensive audits and formal verification of Reward Vault smart contracts to prevent vulnerabilities that could lead to tampering or exploits.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may contain software vulnerabilities that could be exploited.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rigorous audits, formal verification, and enforce secure development practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and formal verification proof.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-signature and time-lock mechanisms for governance actions such as proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.description: Require multiple guardian signatures and enforce timelocks on governance proposals to prevent unauthorized or malicious cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The timelock and multisig mechanisms may be vulnerable if private keys are compromised or implementation flaws exist.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multi-signature wallets with hardware security modules and enforce timelock procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of multisig signatures and timelock transaction records.

checklist_items.title: Verify that all governance proposals are reviewed and approved through multi-party consensus mechanisms.
checklist_items.description: Use multi-party approval and high quorum requirements to mitigate risks of tampering with governance decisions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance proposals lack adequate multi-party approval, leading to risks of internal compromise or unauthorized actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-party approval processes and safeguard governance keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Records of governance approvals with multi-signature attestations.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of validators and guardians with hardware modules and multi-signature wallets.
checklist_items.description: Enhance operational security of validator and guardian private keys to prevent privilege escalation and impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Privileged keys for validators and guardians may be vulnerable to theft or compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules (HSMs), multi-signature schemes, and strict operational protocols.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key access logs, hardware wallet audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict access controls and role management for all smart contract administrative functions.
checklist_items.description: Ensure only authorized personnel or multisignature governance can perform sensitive operations such as pausing, upgrading, or changing parameters in Reward Vaults and other critical contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Administrative functions may be accessible to overly privileged roles or improperly secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use role-based access control (RBAC) with multisig governance and timelocks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control configurations, role assignment logs.

checklist_items.title: Use secure, trusted, and verified RPC endpoints for blockchain interactions.
checklist_items.description: Users should connect only to official RPC servers with transport layer security (HTTPS) to prevent spoofing, MitM, and data tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The user may connect to malicious or compromised RPC endpoints that perform MitM or data tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Build wallet warnings, verify RPC URLs, and promote use of official endpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Wallet connection logs, RPC provider lists.

checklist_items.title: Apply HTTPS and end-to-end encryption for all RPC communications.
checklist_items.description: Prevent traffic interception and tampering between user's wallet and RPC endpoint by enforcing secure communication channels.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RPC communication is vulnerable to MitM if not secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS, use VPNs or private networks, implement TLS for RPC endpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network traffic captures showing TLS encryption in use.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication mechanisms for forum users submitting RFRV proposals
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only verified identities, such as linked on-chain identities, can submit RFRV proposals to prevent spoofing or impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum (Component 223) lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Ensure on-chain verification of Reward Vault whitelisting status before accepting emissions
checklist_items.description: Verify that Reward Vault addresses are whitelisted and active in the smart contract before directing $BGT emissions to prevent diversion to malicious or unapproved vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system (e.g., Distributor Contract 214) might not robustly check the status/validity of a target Reward Vault before attempting to send emissions, or this check could be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Strict on-chain verification that target Reward Vaults are whitelisted and active before emissions are processed.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing validation steps for Reward Vault whitelisting during emission transactions.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of Validator nodes used for signing emission directives and block proposals
checklist_items.description: Use hardware security modules, multi-signature requirements, and operational security best practices to protect Validator private keys against compromise, preventing unauthorized activity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Validator's private key used for signing emission direction transactions can be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure validator key management (e.g., HSMs, robust operational security). Optional: Implement a cooldown period or multi-signature requirement for changing emission directions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audit logs, hardware wallet configuration screenshots.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain validation for validator approval and staking parameters
checklist_items.description: Ensure that validator admission processes and staking thresholds are enforced directly within smart contracts, preventing elevation of privileges through flawed off-chain logic.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There is a vulnerability in the Governance Contracts (212) related to determining voting power (e.g., from $BGT balance/delegation at snapshot) or vote counting.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits of Governance Contracts, formal verification of vote counting and power calculation logic, use of secure and well-tested snapshot mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification records, test case results.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure operational procedures and multi-party consensus for governance proposal execution
checklist_items.description: Require multiple independent approvals and time delays before executing critical governance proposals to reduce risk of malicious or unauthorized actions being carried out.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A malicious actor managed to get a harmful proposal approved by $BGT voters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Thorough review of governance proposals by the community and guardians. Clear explanations of proposal effects. Robust timelock and guardian veto mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Official meeting minutes, multisig transaction logs, proposal approval records.

checklist_items.title: Verify the authenticity of RPC endpoints before establishing wallet connections
checklist_items.description: Users should only connect to official, verified RPC URLs to prevent connection to malicious or spoofed nodes that could intercept or manipulate their transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User (Actor 1) might be tricked into connecting to a fake RPC endpoint, potentially leading to phishing or transaction manipulation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Always use official, bookmarked links. Verify contract addresses before interacting. Wallet warnings for phishing sites.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User account connection logs, browser history, RPC verification records.
checklist_items.owner: User Education and Wallet Support Team

checklist_items.title: Implement HTTPS and certificate pinning for RPC communication channels
checklist_items.description: Ensure all communication between wallets and RPC endpoints is secured with TLS/SSL, and where possible, implement certificate pinning to prevent MitM attacks that could tamper with or eavesdrop on traffic.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Attacker can perform a Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attack if RPC communication is not secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS for all RPC communications. Wallets should verify server certificates. Encourage users to connect via secure networks.
checklist_items.evidence_required: TLS configuration audits, wallet implementation documentation, network security logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify the use of cryptographic signatures for identity verification in RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the process for submitting RFRVs requires cryptographic signatures linked to verified on-chain identities to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing cryptographic signatures in RFRV submissions.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain validation of Reward Vault whitelisting status before distributing $BGT emissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the Reward Vault smart contracts verify their whitelist status on-chain before accepting emissions to prevent directing funds to unauthorized or malicious vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system (e.g., Distributor Contract 214) might not robustly check the status/validity of a target Reward Vault before attempting to send emissions, or this check could be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Integrate on-chain whitelist verification within the emission distribution logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit report showing whitelist checks during emission transactions.

checklist_items.title: Ensure secure management of Validator private keys with hardware security modules or multisignature setups
checklist_items.description: Implement hardware security modules (HSMs) or multisig arrangements for Validator keys used in signing emission and proposal transactions to prevent unauthorized spoofing or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's private key used for signing emission direction transactions can be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure validator key management (e.g., HSMs, robust operational security). Optional: Implement a cooldown period or multi-signature requirement for changing emission directions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Certificate of key management procedures, HSM audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Publish and verify official smart contract addresses for Reward Vaults and Bridge contracts
checklist_items.description: Maintain a publicly accessible and cryptographically signed registry of official contract addresses to prevent users from interacting with spoofed or malicious contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Attackers can deploy smart contracts on Berachain (201) that are designed to look like official Reward Vaults of known protocols.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Protocols should clearly publicize their official smart contract addresses through multiple secure channels.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Official registry documentation, signed cryptographic records.

checklist_items.title: Enforce HTTPS and TLS protocols for all RPC endpoints used by users and wallets
checklist_items.description: Configure RPC endpoints to require HTTPS with valid certificates to prevent MitM tampering and eavesdropping on RPC communication.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Attacker can perform a Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attack to intercept and tamper with RPC requests/responses.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS for all RPC communications.
checklist_items.evidence_required: SSL/TLS certificate audits, network scan reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement user education and wallet warnings for external links and contract addresses
checklist_items.description: Educate users to verify URLs, contract addresses, and transaction details before signing, especially when interacting with dApps and liquidity pools.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User (Actor 1) is tricked into sending assets to a malicious contract believing they are legitimate.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use official, bookmarked links. Verify contract addresses before transaction approval.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training materials, documentation of verification procedures.

checklist_items.title: Deploy and maintain anti-phishing domain monitoring and user alerts for malicious sites
checklist_items.description: Continuously monitor for fake website domains mimicking official platforms and alert users through trusted channels to avoid interaction with spoofed sites.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Attackers can create convincing replicas of the official Honey Swap or HoneyFactory sites.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement anti-phishing measures and user warnings.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Anti-phishing service reports, incident response logs.

checklist_items.title: Set up secure multi-party approval or multisignature requirements for critical protocol functions (e.g., proposal execution, key changes)
checklist_items.description: Require multiple independent approvals for sensitive operations to mitigate elevation of privilege via key compromise or contract flaws.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A flaw in multisig validation allows insufficient signature power for proposal cancellation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-party approval with cryptographically secure multisig schemes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig setup documentation, cryptographic signature audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication for users submitting RFRV proposals
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only verified identities, such as wallet-based authentication or on-chain identity, can submit RFRV proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum (223) does not have strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain verification of all proposal submissions and modifications
checklist_items.description: Verify that all governance proposals are signed and recorded on-chain to prevent tampering or impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Contract (212) does not provide cryptographically non-repudiable record of proposals or modifications.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require on-chain signatures and event logs for all proposal submissions and changes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit trail of signed proposals stored on-chain.

checklist_items.title: Apply strict access controls and multi-signature requirements for administrative actions on Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only authorized, multi-signed staff can execute critical functions like setting incentive rates or pausing vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contracts may have flawed or overly permissive access control mechanisms.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-sig access controls and role-based permissions for all admin functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing multi-sig execution of admin functions.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit and formally verify reward distribution logic in Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Perform security audits and formal verification of reward calculation and distribution smart contracts to prevent manipulation or bugs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults (209) may contain exploitable flaws allowing reward theft.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Subject Reward Vault code to audited development process and formal verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and verification certificates.

checklist_items.title: Enforce secure private key management for Validator keys used in block proposal and emission signing
checklist_items.description: Mandate the use of hardware security modules or multi-sig key management for Validator signing keys to prevent spoofing and privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator keys may be compromised if not securely stored.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, secure multi-party key management, and regular key audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policy documentation and hardware wallet validation logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement multisig and threshold signatures for governance proposal creation and voting
checklist_items.description: Ensure that governance proposals and votes are only valid if signed and authorized by a threshold number of verified authorities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance contract may be vulnerable to threshold bypass or single key compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require multisig signatures with threshold verification for proposals and votes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain multi-signature verification records.

checklist_items.title: Use trusted, verified RPC endpoints and wallet connections for interactions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that users connect to and interact through official RPC URLs and verified dApp endpoints to prevent spoofing and MitM attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may connect to malicious RPC endpoints, enabling data interception or spoofing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Distribute official RPC URLs via secure channels and wallet warnings.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Secure deployment records and user connection logs.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of Validator nodes and key management infrastructure
checklist_items.description: Maintain hardware security modules or multisig key management for validator keys to prevent malicious signing and spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys could be stolen or misused if not secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy keys within HSMs and conduct regular key audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key rotation logs and hardware wallet deployment records.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication for BGT Foundation members submitting RFRV reviews
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the BGT Foundation requires multi-factor authentication or digital signatures to verify the identity of reviewers, preventing impersonation or spoofing attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The BGT Foundation's review process lacks robust mechanisms to verify the authenticity of review submissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce digital signatures or multi-factor authentication for Foundation members' review actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing linked wallet identities or signed review submissions.

checklist_items.title: Require explicit verification of smart contract addresses before user interactions
checklist_items.description: Promote user practices of verifying the smart contract addresses they interact with, such as checking against official sources or using trusted wallets, to prevent spoofing of Reward Vault contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users often do not verify contract addresses, leading to spoofing risks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Educate users and implement verification steps in wallet interfaces and official documentation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training records or wallet UI screenshots showing verification steps.

checklist_items.title: Conduct comprehensive security audits of Reward Vault smart contracts before deployment
checklist_items.description: Ensure all Reward Vault contracts are thoroughly audited by reputable security firms to identify and fix vulnerabilities that could be exploited for tampering, denial of service, or privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may contain exploitable vulnerabilities due to poor development practices.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement mandatory security audits and formal verification processes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and formal verification results.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain authorization checks for critical functions in Reward Vault contracts
checklist_items.description: Implement role-based access control and multi-signature requirements for administrative functions to prevent elevation of privilege attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults may have flawed access control mechanisms that could be exploited.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use established role management libraries and conduct access control audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control configuration and audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement multisignature and timelock mechanisms for governance proposal cancellations
checklist_items.description: Require that proposal cancellations in the Timelock Contract be authorized by multiple Guardians' signatures with a threshold (e.g., 5-of-9) to prevent unauthorized cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Timelock Contract relies on multisignature for proposal cancellations, which could be compromised if signatures are forged or signatures thresholds are bypassed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use well-audited multisig protocols and enforce strict signature verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature verification procedures and multisig setup documentation.

checklist_items.title: Secure off-chain governance review and decision processes with multi-party approvals
checklist_items.description: Establish multi-party and multisignature approval for decisions related to RFRV review outcomes to prevent internal tampering or unilateral decisions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The BGT Foundation's review process could be tampered with if internal decision-making is not properly controlled.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-party approval protocols, and digital signatures for review decisions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operation logs and approval signatures.

checklist_items.title: Implement exclusive monitoring and alerting for governance proposal activities
checklist_items.description: Set up comprehensive on-chain and off-chain monitoring systems to detect suspicious activity (e.g., high proposal volume, unauthorized proposals, rapid approvals) in governance processes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance activities are potentially vulnerable to internal or external manipulation and require detection mechanisms.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy real-time monitoring dashboards and alerting systems with anomaly detection.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring logs and alert reports.

checklist_items.title: Use encrypted communication channels for Guardians coordination and off-chain discussions
checklist_items.description: Require all Guardians' off-chain communications related to proposal cancellations or sensitive governance activities to be conducted over encrypted and authenticated channels to prevent leakage or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Off-chain Guardians' coordination could be intercepted or leaked if not secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce encrypted messaging protocols or secure collaboration platforms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational procedures and communication protocol documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement user education programs on verifying web interface authenticity and contract addresses
checklist_items.description: Educate users to always verify the URLs, official sources, and contract addresses before interacting with DEXs, bridge sites, or claim tools to prevent spoofing and phishing attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users are vulnerable to phishing and fake interfaces.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regular user training and official communication channels.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Training attendance records and user acknowledgment forms.

checklist_items.title: Require transaction signing verification with explicit confirmation of parameters in hardware wallets or secure trusted environments
checklist_items.description: Encourage or enforce the use of hardware wallets that display transaction details prominently, requiring users to verify all parameters before signing, to prevent tampering attacks during swap, stake, or claim transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users might sign altered or malicious transactions due to insufficient verification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Promote hardware wallet usage and wallet UI improvements to display transaction details clearly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Wallet configuration and user verification procedures.

checklist_items.title: Verify strong authentication mechanisms for RFRV submissions to prevent spoofing and impersonation
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the governance process requires wallet-based login or cryptographic signatures from verified on-chain identities when submitting RFRVs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum does not have robust mechanisms to verify the true identity of RFRV submitters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, digital signatures).
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing verified linked identities for RFRV submitters.

checklist_items.title: Implement blockchain-based verification for proposal creation and voting to prevent tampering and impersonation
checklist_items.description: Use cryptographically signed on-chain transactions and multi-signature approvals for governance proposals and votes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance process lacks cryptographic proof linking proposals and votes to verified identities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require signatures from verified token holders or their authorized representatives.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain signatures and multi-sig records verifying proposal and vote authenticity.

checklist_items.title: Set up continuous monitoring for suspicious activities and anomalies in governance proposal submissions and voting patterns
checklist_items.description: Regularly analyze on-chain proposals and votes to identify patterns indicative of tampering or fraudulent activity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Potential for tampered or malicious governance proposals to go unnoticed without monitoring.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement off-chain analytics and on-chain event alerts for anomalies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of regular monitoring reports, alert notifications.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access controls on smart contracts governing reward distribution to prevent unauthorized privilege escalation
checklist_items.description: Apply role-based access control and multi-signature requirements for critical functions like setting incentives and administrative changes in Reward Vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may have flawed or overly permissive access controls.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement role-based access control with multisig governance for admin functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract source code, access control audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Audit and formally verify reward distribution logic in Reward Vaults to prevent exploits and monetary losses
checklist_items.description: Conduct security audits and formal verification of smart contracts handling incentives and rewards to identify and fix vulnerabilities in distribution mechanisms.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults may contain exploitable flaws in incentive calculation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Perform audits by reputable firms and formal verification where feasible.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification documentation.

checklist_items.title: Ensure that the bridge smart contracts have undergone formal security audits and include fail-safe mechanisms
checklist_items.description: Perform formal verification of cross-chain bridge contracts and implement emergency stop or upgrade mechanisms to mitigate Tampering and Exploitation risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The bridge contracts may contain vulnerabilities exploitable for unauthorized minting or asset theft.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct formal audits and deploy upgradeable, timelocked contracts with emergency pause functionality.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit certification, upgrade and pause governance records.

checklist_items.title: Verify strong authentication mechanisms for forum RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure the Governance Forum utilizes wallet-based or multi-factor authentication to confirm that only verified members can submit RFRV proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum (Component 223) lacks strong, verifiable authentication for entities submitting RFRVs.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., digital signatures, verified on-chain identities) for RFRV submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing authentication methods used during submission.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain verification and whitelisting of Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all Reward Vaults (Component 209) are verified and whitelisted on-chain before they are eligible to receive $BGT emissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator (Actor 2) can direct $BGT emissions to non-whitelisted Reward Vaults if checks are inadequate.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce on-chain whitelisting checks for Reward Vaults prior to emissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs demonstrating whitelist checks during emission direction.

checklist_items.title: Secure validator key management and multi-signature controls for emission directives
checklist_items.description: Apply hardware security modules, multi-signature requirements, and operational security best practices to validator keys used for directing $BGT emissions (Component 215).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's private key can be compromised, allowing spoofed emission directives.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use HSMs, enforce multi-signature signing, and conduct operational security reviews.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of key access and signing procedures.

checklist_items.title: Establish robust access controls and role management in reward management contracts
checklist_items.description: Implement role-based access control with least privilege principles in Reward Vaults (Component 209) to prevent unauthorized function calls, especially for administrative actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may have flawed or vulnerable access control mechanisms.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply well-tested access control patterns and enforce strict privileges.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review reports indicating role restriction enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Use multi-factor authentication and hardware wallets for admin and validator accounts
checklist_items.description: Require multi-signature wallets and hardware devices for administrative and validator private keys, especially those involved in controlling reward emissions and key operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Unauthorized privilege escalation due to key compromise is possible.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement hardware wallet integration and multi-signature procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration documentation of key management procedures.

checklist_items.title: Verify correct configuration and deployment of smart contracts before whitelisting
checklist_items.description: Require comprehensive security audits and formal verification of Reward Vault and other smart contracts prior to deployment and whitelisting for interactions involving assets and incentives.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Deployed Reward Vaults may contain vulnerabilities leading to exploits.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct thorough audits and formal verification before deployment.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement Strong Authentication for RFRV Submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only verified identities, such as on-chain wallet-based authentication, can submit RFRV proposals to prevent impersonation and spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Enforce On-Chain Identity Verification for Proposal Creation
checklist_items.description: Require that all RFRV submitters authenticate via on-chain signatures linked to their verified wallet addresses to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The verification of submitter identity is weak in the current forum process.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require digital signatures from wallet addresses associated with verified identities during proposal submission.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal submission transactions or logs demonstrating signature verification.

checklist_items.title: Validate Reward Vault Whitelisting Prior to Emission Direction
checklist_items.description: Implement on-chain checks to verify that Reward Vaults are whitelisted and active before directing $BGT emissions to them to prevent misallocation and denial of rewards.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator can direct emissions without verifying vault status.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: On-chain whitelist verification and status checks before emission transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing on-chain whitelist status at the time of emission direction.

checklist_items.title: Secure Validator Private Keys and Implement Multi-Signature Controls
checklist_items.description: Ensure that validator keys used for signing emission and delegation transactions are generated and stored securely, preferably in hardware security modules, with multi-signature authorization for critical operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's keys can be compromised, enabling impersonation or unauthorized transaction signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets and multisig schemes for private key management; conduct regular key audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audit logs and hardware wallet configurations.

checklist_items.title: Implement Role-Based Access Control with Multi-Factor Authentication for Governance System
checklist_items.description: Enforce strict access controls for BGT Foundation members, requiring multi-factor authentication for critical actions like proposal review, approval, and revocation to prevent privilege escalation and internal tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak access controls within the governance system enable privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: RBAC policies combined with MFA and regular access audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control policy documents and authentication logs.

checklist_items.title: Use Cryptographically Signed and Verified Messages for Proposal and Vote Attribution
checklist_items.description: Require that all proposal submissions and votes be accompanied by cryptographic signatures from verified on-chain identities to establish non-repudiable proof of origin and prevent internal tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Lack of cryptographic proof causes threats of repudiation and false attribution.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Mandate signatures from official identities and verify on-chain signatures during proposal and vote processing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed message payloads and verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong user authentication for RFRV submissions.
checklist_items.description: Verify that the governance platform requires users to authenticate via their verified on-chain identity or multi-factor authentication before submitting RFRVs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce wallet-based login, digital signatures, and multi-factor authentication for proposal submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing verified user identities linked to submissions.

checklist_items.title: Secure and verify contract addresses in staking, bonding, and voting transactions.
checklist_items.description: Ensure users verify the contract addresses before signing transactions related to staking or governance actions, ideally through wallet UI warnings or verified address lists.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The user does not meticulously verify the raw transaction data of the vote or stake in their wallet.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement address verification checks in user wallets and educate users on verifying contract addresses.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of transaction details verified by user before signing.

checklist_items.title: Use secure private key management practices for all validators and governance signers.
checklist_items.description: Implement hardware wallets, multi-signature controls, and operational security protocols to protect private keys used for governance and signing emission or transaction directives.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's private keys are potentially vulnerable to theft or compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy hardware security modules (HSMs), require multi-factor authentication, and conduct regular key audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs from key management systems demonstrating secure key storage.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive input validation and transaction sanity checks in user interfaces and smart contracts.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all user inputs, especially for bridging, token swaps, and reward claims, are validated on both client-side and smart contract levels before processing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Client-side or off-chain components may be compromised, leading to tampered transactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Smart contract validation routines, UI validation prompts, and thorough input sanitization.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Test reports demonstrating input validation mechanisms.

checklist_items.title: Establish multi-signature or timelock controls for critical governance and administrative actions.
checklist_items.description: Require multiple guardians or a time delay for proposal cancellations, parameter changes, or administrative privileges to prevent unilateral malicious actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Guardian multisig or timelock mechanisms may be vulnerable to key compromise or insufficient thresholds.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-party signatures, enforce multisig thresholds, and ensure timelock parameters are set appropriately.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of multisig transactions and timelock configurations.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit smart contracts and perform formal verification for critical components.
checklist_items.description: Conduct security audits and formal verification on reward vaults, bridge contracts, and governance contracts to identify and fix vulnerabilities before deployment.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults and bridge contracts may contain exploitable vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable auditors, implement formal verification tools, and continue bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and verified formal proofs.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strong authentication for Governance Forum submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the Governance Forum requires users to authenticate using cryptographically secure methods linked to on-chain identities before submitting RFRV proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing verified on-chain identities associated with forum submissions.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain verification of Reward Vault whitelisting status
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the reward distribution contracts verify the Reward Vault's whitelisting status on-chain before depositing incentives or executing reward transfers.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A validator can direct $BGT emissions to non-whitelisted or faulty Reward Vaults without proper on-chain verification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Add on-chain whitelisting checks in Reward Vault and distributor contracts before processing emissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code audit reports confirming whitelisting verification logic is enforced.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-factor authentication for validator key operations
checklist_items.description: Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) and secure key management practices for validator keys used in signing emission directives and other privileged transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Validator's private key used for signing emission direction transactions can be compromised, enabling spoofing of emissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules (HSM), multisignature schemes, and enforce operational security protocols for key storage.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of key access and signing activities, verification of secure hardware wallet deployment.

checklist_items.title: Implement safe transaction verification procedures in user wallets
checklist_items.description: Encourage or enforce that users verify transaction details explicitly in their wallets before signing, especially for critical actions like staking, redeeming, or bridging assets.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The user does not meticulously verify all transaction details in their wallet before signing complex or high-value transactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Educate users on transaction verification, and wallet features that display all relevant details clearly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training materials, wallet UI/UX reviews showing clear transaction summaries.
checklist_items.owner: User Education and Wallet Development Teams

checklist_items.title: Ensure secure communication protocols for RPC endpoints
checklist_items.description: Use HTTPS or other secure transport protocols for all RPC communication to prevent MitM tampering and eavesdropping.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RPC communication with the Berachain node may be intercepted or modified if not secured properly.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Mandate HTTPS or other secure transport protocols for RPC endpoints, and notify users to connect only through trusted nodes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration audits showing use of TLS/SSL, network monitoring logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication mechanisms for RFRV submissions to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Governance Forum employs robust identity verification, such as linking accounts to on-chain identities or using multi-factor authentication, to ensure submitters are verified.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Systems or processes acting on BGT Foundation's review decisions lack robust mechanisms to verify the authenticity of such decisions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., digital signatures, dedicated secure channels) for all official communications and actions taken by the BGT Foundation. Publicly document official communication channels.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing signed and verifiable BGT Foundation decisions with cryptographic proof.

checklist_items.title: Enforce whitelist checks before executing reward emissions to Reward Vaults.
checklist_items.description: Verify that the RewardDistributor smart contracts perform on-chain whitelisting checks for Reward Vaults before directing $BGT emissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A validator might maliciously or accidentally direct emissions to non-functional or non-whitelisted vaults.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict on-chain verification that target Reward Vaults are whitelisted and active before emissions are processed. Monitor for emissions sent to unwhitelisted vaults.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing emissions only directed to verified, whitelisted vaults.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of validators and role accounts managing reward distributions.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all private keys used by validators and reward managers are protected using hardware security modules, multi-signature schemes, and operational security best practices to prevent unauthorized signing of malicious transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator's private key used for signing emission direction transactions can be compromised, enabling spoofing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure validator key management (e.g., HSMs, blockchain wallet keys protected by hardware). Implement multi-signature controls for reward distributions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of key access, hardware wallet reports, multi-signature transaction records.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict on-chain verification for Reward Vaults' whitelist status before emitting rewards.
checklist_items.description: Verify that the reward emission logic includes on-chain checks confirming the Reward Vault is whitelisted and active before distribution.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A validator might maliciously or accidentally direct emissions to non-whitelisted vaults.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Integrate on-chain whitelist verification checks into emission functions. Monitor for emissions to unverified vaults.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs demonstrating emissions only to whitelisted vaults.

checklist_items.title: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of Reward Vault smart contracts.
checklist_items.description: Regularly audit Reward Vault contracts for vulnerabilities, especially in access control, fund management, and inflation logic, and consider formal verification methods.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may contain vulnerabilities; attackers can exploit flaws.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement rigorous security audits by reputable firms, conduct formal verification where feasible, and establish bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification proofs, bug bounty reports.

checklist_items.title: Use hardware wallets and verify all transaction details before signing, especially for sensitive operations like staking, unstaking, claiming, or redeems.
checklist_items.description: Educate and enforce that users verify transaction parameters on trusted wallets with hardware security features before approving any transaction involving assets.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User's wallet or interaction environment could be compromised, allowing tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, verify transaction details, and follow best security practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User reports, transaction screenshots showing the verified details.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strong cryptographic authentication for Initial Core Contributors claiming vested tokens
checklist_items.description: Implement cryptographic signatures or wallet-based authentication to verify the identity of contributors during token claim processes, mitigating spoofing risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The authentication mechanism for claiming vested tokens is weak or relies solely on private key secrecy.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce cryptographic signatures and wallet-based authentication to verify token claim identity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs demonstrating use of cryptographic signatures during claims.

checklist_items.title: Secure storage and management of private keys for EOA actors
checklist_items.description: Require initiators of token claims to use hardware wallets or secure signing environments to prevent private key theft, reducing spoofing success.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Private keys associated with contributor EOAs can be compromised through phishing, malware, or other attack vectors.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement hardware wallet enforcement and educate contributors on private key security.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Certification or audit report of key management practices.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain confidentiality measures for contributor allocation details
checklist_items.description: Minimize or obfuscate storage of detailed individual vesting schedules and allocations on-chain to protect contributor privacy and prevent information disclosure threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Public access to detailed vesting schedules could be unintentionally exposing contributor information.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use aggregation, hashing, or Merkle trees to conceal detailed vesting data stored on-chain.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Design documentation demonstrating on-chain data obfuscation methods.

checklist_items.title: Audit smart contracts for denial-of-service vectors and implement safeguards
checklist_items.description: Conduct security audits focusing on potential reentrancy, gas griefing, and upgradeability issues in vesting, reward, and bridge smart contracts, and implement circuit breakers or upgrade paths.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Vulnerabilities in vesting, reward, or bridge contracts could prevent user operations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Regular audits, formal verification, and emergency pause mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports with identified vulnerabilities and mitigation measures.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access controls and multisig governance for contract administrative functions
checklist_items.description: Implement role-based access control, multisignature management, and timelocks for administrative operations in reward vaults and bridge contracts to prevent elevation of privilege vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward vaults and bridge contracts may contain administrative functions vulnerable to privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multisignature wallets, role-based permissions, and timelocked critical functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review and deployment policies confirming access controls.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure private key management and operational security for validator keys
checklist_items.description: Utilize hardware security modules, cold storage, and operational security best practices to protect validator signing keys used for consensus and emission signing, reducing privilege escalation threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator keys are vulnerable to theft or misuse, leading to elevation of privilege or spoofing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce hardware wallet usage, multi-party signing, and strict access controls for validator key operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policy documentation and hardware wallet deployment records.

checklist_items.title: Regular security audits and formal verification of consensus and validator logic
checklist_items.description: Perform comprehensive audits of Berachain's consensus, validator, and block proposal logic to identify and mitigate tampering, spoofing, and privilege escalation vectors.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Potential flaws in validator proposal and consensus mechanisms could be exploited for spoofing or tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Integrate formal verification tools and periodic audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and formal verification evidence.

checklist_items.title: Verify strong authentication for BGT Foundation members involved in RFRV review process
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only verified and authenticated members can submit or approve RFRV proposals to prevent spoofing and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Systems or processes acting on BGT Foundation's review decisions lack robust mechanisms to verify the authenticity of such decisions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., digital signatures, wallet-based verification) for all official communications and decisions from the BGT Foundation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs or signed messages proving Foundation approvals.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-signature or threshold authorization for proposal cancellations
checklist_items.description: Require multiple Guardian signatures (e.g., 5 of 9) for proposal cancellation, to prevent privilege escalation through signature forgery or key compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Timelock Contract correctly requires 5-of-9 signatures for cancellation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Design multisig with proven multi-signature schemes, enforce threshold validation in the contract.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain multisig signature validation reports or audit results.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of Validator operators using hardware security modules or multisig arrangements
checklist_items.description: Ensure that Validator private keys used for block proposal and signing are stored securely, employing hardware wallets or multisig setups to prevent spoofing or tampering with block proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A compromised validator key allows block spoofing or tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules (HSMs), hardware wallets, or multisig custody solutions for validator keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policy documentation, hardware wallet audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify whitelist status of Reward Vaults before directing emissions or incentives
checklist_items.description: Implement on-chain checks to validate that Reward Vaults are whitelisted and active before sending $BGT emissions, to prevent diversion or denial of rewards.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator can direct emissions to non-whitelisted or malicious vaults.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce whitelist validation in emission contract logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code audit reports showing whitelist enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure transaction signing practices for Validator keys
checklist_items.description: Require hardware wallets or secure signing environments for Validator keystream operations to prevent tampering with emission directives or reward distributions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A validator's private key compromise enables spoofing emission or reward transactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Mandate hardware wallet use and multi-factor authentication for key operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational procedures, key storage audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Use HTTPS/TLS for all RPC connections and verify server certificates
checklist_items.description: Ensure all user and node communications with RPC endpoints (Component 220) are encrypted with TLS, and clients verify server certificates to prevent MitM tampering or impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User connects to a malicious RPC endpoint that performs MitM attacks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Configure wallets and clients to enforce TLS and certificate verification, use only official RPC URLs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network logs demonstrating TLS usage and certificate validation.

checklist_items.title: Implement domain and address verification for all dApp interfaces and smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Verify that all user interfaces (Components 208, 217) and smart contracts used for minting, redeeming, or staking are from official sources and have verified contract addresses to prevent spoofing and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Attacker deploys a malicious contract mimicking the real one.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Maintain a registry of official contract addresses and display them prominently in the UI. Encourage users to verify contract addresses before interaction.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code repositories, official communication channels, and UI verification steps.

checklist_items.title: Advertise official contract addresses and educate users on verifying smart contract authenticity
checklist_items.description: Provide clear communication and guidelines to users about how to identify and verify official smart contract addresses, to prevent interaction with spoofed or malicious contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may interact with malicious or unverified contracts.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Publish and promote official contract addresses, tutorials, and warnings.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Public messaging campaigns, user feedback, and awareness metrics.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication linking forum accounts to on-chain identities
checklist_items.description: Verify if forum accounts submitting RFRVs are authenticated via on-chain identities to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain verification for RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that RFRV submissions are validated through on-chain verification of the submitter's identity and legitimacy.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The process for submitting RFRVs relies solely on off-chain forum posts without on-chain verification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Integrate on-chain verification steps into the RFRV submission process, such as digital signatures or on-chain attestations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing the on-chain verification process for RFRV submissions.

checklist_items.title: Secure the off-chain proof generation infrastructure for incentive claims
checklist_items.description: Implement strong security controls for the off-chain backend service that generates proofs for incentive claims to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The backend service generating proofs for BGTIncentiveDistributor is compromised or has vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure the backend proof generation service, implement strong validation of proofs within the smart contract, ensure transparency and auditability of the proof generation process, consider decentralized proof mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review reports, audit reports, or logs demonstrating security controls on the proof generation infrastructure.

checklist_items.title: Implement multisignature and timelock controls for critical governance actions
checklist_items.description: Use multisignature approval and timelock mechanisms to prevent unauthorized or malicious proposal cancellations and governance actions.
