checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Timelock Contract might incorrectly validate a cancellation signal if enough Guardian keys are compromised and used to spoof a legitimate multisig action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust private key security measures for all Guardians. Monitor for suspicious Guardian activity. Ensure the multisig contract logic is sound and audited.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig transaction logs, audit reports, or security review documents demonstrating control mechanisms.

checklist_items.title: Conduct regular security audits and formal verification of Reward Vault contracts
checklist_items.description: Perform security audits and formal verification of Reward Vault smart contracts before deployment to prevent vulnerabilities that could allow tampering or exploits.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault smart contract deployed by a Protocol contains exploitable flaws (e.g., reentrancy, integer overflow/underflow, incorrect access control).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Mandate rigorous security audits by reputable firms for all Reward Vault contracts before they can be whitelisted. Promote secure development practices and use of standardized, audited libraries. Implement bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, verification certificates, or security assessment documents.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure private key management practices for Validator operators
checklist_items.description: Ensure Validator private keys are stored securely, using hardware security modules (HSMs) or multi-signature schemes, and are protected against theft or compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Validator's private key is compromised, allowing an attacker to spoof block proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure validator key management (e.g., HSMs, robust operational security). Optional: Implement a cooldown period or multi-signature requirement for changing emission directions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policy documents, key access logs, or certification evidence.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong best practices for RPC endpoint security and verification
checklist_items.description: Users should connect only to trusted RPC endpoints and verify the URL and certificates before interaction to prevent spoofing and MitM attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User connects to an untrusted or malicious RPC endpoint, leading to impersonation or data tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS for all RPC communications. Users should verify the authenticity of RPC URLs and certificates. Wallet warnings for suspicious endpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Proof of connection to trusted RPC endpoints, browser security settings, or wallet configuration screenshots.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong on-chain verification for RFRV submission authorship
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only verified and authenticated users or entities can submit RFRV proposals, linking submissions to verified on-chain identities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: System currently lacks strong verification mechanisms linking forum submissions to on-chain identities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require cryptographic signatures or blockchain-based identity verification for RFRV submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing submission signatures and linked on-chain identities.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access controls and audit logs for governance proposal modifications
checklist_items.description: Maintain comprehensive and tamper-proof logs of all governance proposal creations, modifications, and executions to prevent and trace tampering attempts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance system currently does not ensure non-repudiation of proposal actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy on-chain event logs with cryptographic signatures and multi-party approvals for proposal modifications.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Immutable audit trail records of proposal lifecycle events.

checklist_items.title: Verify whitelist status before directing incentives to Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Implement on-chain checks to confirm that Reward Vaults receiving incentives are approved and whitelisted before executing reward emission transactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator or incentive distributor may direct rewards to non-whitelisted vaults due to lack of verification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require on-chain whitelist verification in reward distribution contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing reward emissions only to whitelisted vaults.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-signature and timelock mechanisms for administration functions of Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Securing admin functions such as pausing, updating, or upgrading Reward Vaults with multi-signature approvals and timelocks to prevent unauthorized or rushed privileged actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Admin controls in Reward Vaults may be vulnerable to privilege escalation or malicious actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy multi-sig governance and timelock contracts for admin functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports of admin function signatures and timelock logs.

checklist_items.title: Conduct regular security audits and formal verification of Reward Vault contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure Reward Vault smart contracts undergo thorough audits and formal verification to identify and fix vulnerabilities related to reentrancy, access control, and other common exploits.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may contain exploitable vulnerabilities that could be maliciously exploited.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Commission audits by reputable security firms and pursue formal verification where applicable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, verification certificates, and formal proof documents.

checklist_items.title: Verify that forum submission system enforces strong user authentication linked to on-chain identity
checklist_items.description: Check if the Governance Forum requires users to authenticate via their blockchain wallet to prevent impersonation or spoofing of RFRV submitters.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Ensure Reward Vault smart contracts are thoroughly audited and formally verified before deployment
checklist_items.description: Conduct rigorous security audits focusing on access control, logic flaws, and reentrancy vulnerabilities in Reward Vault contracts to prevent tampering or malicious modifications.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract deployed by a Protocol (Actor 5) contains exploitable flaws (e.g., reentrancy, integer overflow/underflow, incorrect access control).
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits for all Reward Vault contracts, formal verification if possible, secure and validated reward payout mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification certificates, or code review documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain whitelisting and validation for Reward Vaults receiving emissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure the system checks the whitelist and vault status on-chain before directing emissions, preventing directing funds to unapproved or malicious vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The validator might maliciously or accidentally direct emissions to a non-whitelisted or faulty Reward Vault.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce strict on-chain verification that target Reward Vaults are whitelisted and active before emissions are processed.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs, smart contract code, and on-chain whitelist records.

checklist_items.title: Ensure all private keys of validators and Reward Vault administrators are securely managed with multi-signature and hardware wallets
checklist_items.description: Prevent privilege escalation or unauthorized control by enforcing secure key management practices for entities with administrative or validator privileges.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The private keys controlling admin functions or signing emission directives can be compromised or are not sufficiently secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure validator key management using hardware wallets, multisig setups, operational security best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policy documentation, security audit reports, hardware wallet deployment records.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure transaction signing protocols with user-verification of transaction details
checklist_items.description: Ensure users verify all parameters (amounts, addresses, functions) in their wallets before signing, especially during minting, redeeming, or staking activities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User does not verify transaction details before signing, making them susceptible to tampering or malicious transactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Educate users on verifying all transaction parameters. Use wallet features that display transaction details prominently.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training materials, wallet UI screenshots showing transaction details.
checklist_items.owner: User Education and Wallet Ops Team

checklist_items.title: Ensure off-chain proof generation services are secured and auditable
checklist_items.description: Deploy the backend proof services with strong security controls, regular audits, and transparency measures to prevent tampering that could lead to fraudulent incentive claims.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The off-chain proof generation service could be compromised or have vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure backend infrastructure, enforce strong access controls, conduct regular security audits, and consider decentralizing proof mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, deployment configurations, cryptographic proof logs.

checklist_items.title: Use encrypted and verified communication channels for all RPC interactions
checklist_items.description: Ensure users connect to RPC endpoints over HTTPS or trusted VPNs, verify endpoint URLs, and avoid using untrusted or unknown RPC service providers.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Communication with RPC endpoints is susceptible to MitM or tampering if not secured.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement HTTPS or TLS for RPC endpoint access and educate users about verifying endpoint authenticity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration audit logs, SSL/TLS certificates, user verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Maintain comprehensive audit logs with cryptographic signatures for governance proposals and decisions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all BGT Foundation governance proposals, reviews, and decisions are digitally signed and stored in an immutable, verifiable manner to prevent repudiation and facilitate accountability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance review process lacks non-repudiation measures, enabling actors to deny actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain or cryptographically signed audit trails for all governance activities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs with signatures, blockchain transaction records, or signed documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication linking forum submissions to on-chain identities
checklist_items.description: Check if forum users submitting RFRVs are verified through on-chain identity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain verification of Reward Vault whitelisting status before distributions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the system verifies the whitelist status of Reward Vaults on-chain before directing emissions to prevent directing funds to unverified or malicious vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system (e.g., Distributor Contract 214) might not robustly check the status/validity of a target Reward Vault before sending emissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain verification that Reward Vaults are whitelisted and actively authorized before distribution.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing emission transactions only sent to whitelisted vaults; smart contract source code with whitelist check.

checklist_items.title: Secure validator key management and enforce minimum validator qualification in staking contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure validator private keys are managed with hardware security modules or multi-party control, and enforce strict minimum stake requirements on-chain to prevent malicious validator creation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The staking logic in Berachain (201) might have flaws in enforcing minimum staking amounts or other validator criteria.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure private keys with hardware wallets or HSMs, enforce minimum stake via smart contract logic and audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of validator onboarding; smart contract source code enforcing minimum requirements.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-signature and timelock controls for administrative functions in reward vault contracts
checklist_items.description: Apply multi-signature authorization and timelock mechanisms for critical admin functions (e.g., changing parameters) in Reward Vaults to prevent privilege escalation or malicious control.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract may have a flaw in its authorization logic, allowing unauthorized users to perform admin actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multisig wallets with timelocks for admin controls, conduct rigorous audits of access control logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit reports; multisig transaction logs.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit smart contracts for Denial of Service vectors and critical vulnerabilities
checklist_items.description: Conduct security audits focusing on reentrancy, gas exhaustion, administrative control vulnerabilities, and other DoS vectors in Reward Vaults, Bridge contracts, and governance modules.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault, Bridge, and governance contracts are susceptible to common smart contract vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Schedule regular audits with reputable security firms, implement automated static analysis tools.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, security scan results.

checklist_items.title: Implement user education and UI warnings for phishing and spoofing sites
checklist_items.description: Advise users to verify URLs, smart contract addresses, and to only interact with official interfaces for staking, swapping, bridging, and claiming rewards.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users cannot always reliably distinguish phishing sites from legitimate ones.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Update user guides, run educational campaigns, implement wallet warnings for suspicious sites.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User feedback surveys, wallet warning logs.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure communication channels and operational protocols for guardians and BGT Foundation members
checklist_items.description: Ensure that off-chain discussions, decision-making, and coordination (e.g., governance review, proposal discussions) occur over encrypted, auditable, and authenticated channels to prevent information leakage and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Guardians coordinate off-chain, which could be leaked or tampered.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use encrypted messaging platforms with access controls, enforce operational security policies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Communication logs, security policy documents.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain attribution and non-repudiation mechanisms for governance decisions
checklist_items.description: Use cryptographic signatures, group signatures, or multisig approvals to verify and record accountability for governance proposals and review decisions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance decision records are not inherently non-repudiable.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposals to be signed by authorized entities; store signatures on-chain.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain proposal signatures; audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication mechanisms for forum users submitting RFRV proposals
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Governance Forum requires wallet-based login or cryptographic signatures from verified identities to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum (223) does not have robust mechanisms to verify the true identity of RFRV submitters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, digital signatures)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing authenticated identities associated with submission accounts.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain verification of Reward Vaults before awarding emissions
checklist_items.description: Check that the reward distribution process verifies that Reward Vaults (Component 209) are whitelisted and active before directing $BGT emissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A validator might maliciously or accidentally direct emissions to non-whitelisted or unsuitable vaults.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict on-chain verification of vault whitelist status during emission transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing whitelist checks during emission distribution.

checklist_items.title: Secure private key management for Validator nodes and key holders
checklist_items.description: Ensure private keys used for signing emission directives and critical operations are stored in hardware security modules (HSMs) or multi-signature setups.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator's private key can be compromised, allowing spoofed emission directives.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, HSMs, and multi-party signing procedures for private key storage.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of key access and signing procedures.

checklist_items.title: Validate transaction parameters explicitly in wallet interfaces before signing
checklist_items.description: Ensure wallet UI displays all critical transaction details (amounts, addresses, parameters) for user verification prior to signing, especially for swaps, stakes, or migrations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User does not meticulously verify all transaction details in their wallet before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Design wallet interfaces to show transaction details clearly and require explicit user confirmation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of confirmed transaction details before signing.

checklist_items.title: Implement domain and address verification for all smart contract interactions
checklist_items.description: Verify that users are interacting with official contract addresses and domains, and that the interfaces use HTTPS with valid certificates to prevent spoofing and man-in-the-middle attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User cannot reliably verify the authenticity of the bridge's web interface or smart contract address.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use official URLs, verify contract addresses, and educate users about phishing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports of domain registration and SSL certificates, user guidance documentation.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure communication channels and integrity checks for on-chain governance or proposal data
checklist_items.description: Use cryptographic signatures, multi-signature approvals, or on-chain verification to ensure that governance proposals and review decisions are authentic and non-repudiable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak attribution or non-repudiable logs for review decisions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement cryptographic signing and on-chain recording of governance actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal and decision logs stored on-chain.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit and patch Reward Vault smart contracts to prevent vulnerabilities and bugs
checklist_items.description: Conduct formal security audits, employ best development practices, and implement upgradeability controls with timelocks for any Reward Vault (Component 209) smart contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may contain exploitable flaws.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Scheduled audits, use of established libraries, and governance-controlled upgrades.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, upgrade logs, verification of code integrity.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication and role-based access control for governance and administrative accounts
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all privileged accounts related to governance, minting, or admin functions use multi-factor authentication and are restricted by the principle of least privilege.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak access controls allow privilege escalation or unauthorized actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce MFA, role restrictions, regular access reviews.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs, security policy documents.

checklist_items.title: Enforce cryptographic signatures for all governance proposals to establish verifiable authorship
checklist_items.description: Require that all proposals submitted to the governance system are signed with the submitter's wallet key to ensure authenticity and non-repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance proposal system does not currently verify the authorship of submissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement signature verification for proposals on-chain or via a trusted off-chain authority.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Sample signed proposal submission logs showing signature validation.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for BGT Foundation members with review privileges
checklist_items.description: Require BGT Foundation members to use MFA methods (e.g., hardware wallets, TOTP tokens) when accessing governance review and decision systems to prevent privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The BGT Foundation uses systems that may be vulnerable to credential compromise, risking unauthorized influence on review decisions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require MFA for all admin and review account logins and operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing MFA enforcement for critical system access.

checklist_items.title: Verify that RPC endpoints used by users support HTTPS and certificate pinning
checklist_items.description: Ensure all user-facing RPC endpoints for wallet connections are secured with HTTPS and have certificate pinning to prevent MitM attacks that could tamper with transaction data.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users connect to RPC endpoints without verification, risking MitM tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Mandate HTTPS connections with certificate pinning and instruct users on verifying RPC security.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration audits of RPC endpoints and user connection logs.

checklist_items.title: Maintain a whitelist of official smart contract addresses for reward vaults and bridge contracts
checklist_items.description: Publish and regularly update a public list of verified, audited contract addresses used for reward distribution and asset bridging to prevent users from interacting with spoofed or malicious contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may interact with unverified, rogue contracts if there is no authoritative source for legitimate addresses.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Provide a maintained, authoritative registry of contract addresses and promote verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Snapshot of the published whitelist; logs of address verification during transactions.

checklist_items.title: Audit Reward Vault and Bridge smart contracts for vulnerabilities such as reentrancy, overflows, and access control flaws
checklist_items.description: Engage reputable security firms to perform thorough audits of all smart contracts involved in reward vault functions and asset bridging to identify exploitable vulnerabilities before deployment.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward vault and bridge contracts are deployed without prior comprehensive security audits.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require audits and formal verification prior to contract deployment and whitelisting.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification documentation, and incident history.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction signature verification with out-of-band validation for critical actions such as $BGT emission directives and incentive setting
checklist_items.description: Require that sensitive on-chain actions, especially those changing emission rates or transferring assets, are accompanied by signatures validated through secure, multi-party or multi-signature processes, possibly backed by trusted off-chain validation or multisig custody.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Emission and incentive actions rely solely on single signatures without multi-party validation, increasing risk of spoofing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Adopt multi-signature or threshold signature schemes validated via secure off-chain or on-chain mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature validation logs, multisig approval records, and audit trail of emission setting activities.

checklist_items.title: Ensure all governance proposals are reviewed by multiple independent Guardians or governance members with multi-party approval processes
checklist_items.description: Implement multi-party approval workflows or multi-signature requirements for formulating and executing governance proposals to prevent single points of failure or insider tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance proposals may be approved by a single individual or insufficient oversight, risking malicious proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require multi-member consensus or multisig approval for proposal submission and execution.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Approval logs, multi-signature transaction records, and governance process documentation.

checklist_items.title: Regularly update and audit the list of whitelisted Reward Vaults and check contracts against this list before attaching emissions
checklist_items.description: Implement on-chain or off-chain verification steps that validate Reward Vault addresses before associating them with emissions, ensuring they are whitelisted and verified.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Emission directives may target unverified vaults if the whitelist is outdated or not enforced.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Maintain a cryptographically signed list of verified vault addresses and enforce address verification in emission transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing whitelist enforcement during emission allocation.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication for forum RFRV submissions, linking identities to on-chain credentials
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Governance Forum requires users to authenticate using cryptographic signatures or linked on-chain identities to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing verified user identities linked to on-chain credentials.

checklist_items.title: Ensure contract addresses and parameters are verified before user signing during token swaps and redeem processes
checklist_items.description: Verify that wallet transaction details for swaps and redemptions display the correct contract addresses, amounts, and parameters before signing, to mitigate tampering risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User does not meticulously verify all transaction details in their wallet before signing a swap or redemption transaction.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, wallet UI should clearly display all transaction details for user verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of transaction details before signing, wallet logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) and secure private key management for validators and administrators
checklist_items.description: Enforce MFA and hardware security modules for all validator and admin keys controlling critical functions like stake, propose blocks, and mint/redeem assets.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's or admin's private keys are susceptible to compromise due to inadequate security measures.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, multisig controls, and regular key audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key access logs, hardware wallet audits.

checklist_items.title: Establish end-to-end signed and verified governance proposals and votes
checklist_items.description: Require digital signatures on all governance proposals and votes, and verify signatures on-chain to prevent tampering or impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance process does not require cryptographic proof of authorship for proposals or votes.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain verification of signed proposals and votes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain signature verification logs, signed proposal submissions.

checklist_items.title: Configure and enforce access control policies with least privilege on reward vault and incentive management contracts
checklist_items.description: Restrict administrative functions to authorized roles, prevent unauthorized caller actions, and audit access logs regularly.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may have flawed or overly permissive access controls allowing unauthorized modifications.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement role-based access control with multisig and timelock controls.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs, smart contract code with role restrictions.

checklist_items.title: Conduct regular security audits and formal verification of smart contracts before deployment and after updates
checklist_items.description: Ensure all smart contracts, especially reward vaults, reward distributors, and bridge contracts, are audited for vulnerabilities and formally verified where feasible.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults and bridge contracts may contain vulnerabilities that can be exploited.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Independent security audits and formal verification processes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, verification certificates.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure multi-party approval (multisig) for critical governance and operational functions
checklist_items.description: Require multi-signature approvals for actions like proposal cancellation, parameter changes, or contract upgrades to prevent single point of compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Critical governance actions can be performed by single signatures, which may be vulnerable to key compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multisig schemes with threshold signatures and audit trails.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig approval logs, on-chain signatures.

checklist_items.title: Use trusted and verified RPC endpoints, and verify all RPC URLs before connecting wallets
checklist_items.description: Instruct users to connect only to official RPC URLs, and implement domain verification checks within wallets or dApps to prevent connection to malicious RPC endpoints.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may connect to malicious RPCs if not properly verified.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use only official RPC endpoints, employ domain verification, and educate users.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Wallet connection logs, RPC connection verifications.

checklist_items.title: Monitor and alert on suspicious activities, such as abnormal transaction volumes, failed transaction patterns, or unusual validation failures
checklist_items.description: Deploy monitoring systems and set up alerts for anomalous activities in smart contract interactions, governance proposals, or bridge operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Malicious activities or vulnerabilities could be exploited unnoticed without monitoring.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy real-time monitoring, anomaly detection, and alerting systems.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitor logs, alert reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication for forum users submitting RFRVs
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Governance Forum requires wallet-based authentication or cryptographic signatures to ensure submitters are verified and accountable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum (component 223) lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., link to verified on-chain identity, digital signatures)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing verified user identities linked to forum submissions.

checklist_items.title: Mandate on-chain logging and audit trails for governance proposals and votes
checklist_items.description: Ensure all governance proposal creations and votes are recorded on-chain with cryptographic proof to prevent repudiation and enable accountability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance proposal and voting process may lack immutable on-chain records to prove actions taken.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce that all proposal and vote activities are recorded as on-chain transactions with cryptographic signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain transaction logs and signed proposal/vote records.

checklist_items.title: Secure off-chain communication channels for Guardians' coordination
checklist_items.description: Establish encrypted and access-controlled channels for Guardian communication regarding proposal cancellations to prevent premature disclosure or tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Guardians coordinate or discuss potential cancellations off-chain before executing multisig transactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure, encrypted communication (e.g., Signal, PGP) and operational security best practices for Guardians.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Secure communication logs or protocols demonstrating encrypted Guardian coordination.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for BGT Foundation members
checklist_items.description: Require MFA for all members involved in off-chain review or decision-making to prevent impersonation or privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak access controls or compromised accounts within the BGT Foundation could allow an attacker to manipulate review outcomes.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce MFA, role-based access control, and regular security audits for Foundation members.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs showing MFA-enabled login sessions.

checklist_items.title: Verify contract addresses and highlight official sources for user interactions
checklist_items.description: Ensure the frontend dApps clearly display and verify contract addresses, and instruct users to interact only with verified sources.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may interact with malicious or fake contract addresses if not properly verified.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Display verified contract addresses prominently and educate users on verification procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of dApp showing verified contract addresses and user confirmation logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain approval checks for Reward Vault whitelisting
checklist_items.description: Design the Reward Vault or whitelisting process to require on-chain validation that a vault is whitelisted before allowing $BGT emissions or interactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Emissions are directed without proper on-chain validation of whitelist status, enabling malicious vaults.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce whitelist check within the emission and interaction smart contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain transaction logs showing whitelist verification code execution.

checklist_items.title: Regular security audits and formal verification of governance and reward distribution contracts
checklist_items.description: Subject all governance and reward-related contracts to rigorous audits and formal verification to identify and fix potential vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Vulnerabilities in governance and reward contracts may be exploited if not properly audited.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of critical contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and formal verification proofs.

checklist_items.title: Use reputable, verified RPC endpoints and avoid untrusted sources during wallet connections
checklist_items.description: Encourage users to connect only to RPC nodes that are verified and operated by trusted parties to prevent Man-in-the-Middle and data tampering attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users might connect to malicious or logging RPC endpoints that can intercept or alter data.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Distribute verified RPC URLs and educate users on the importance of verifying endpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User connection logs and user reports of RPC sources.

checklist_items.title: Enforce transport layer security (TLS) for all RPC communications
checklist_items.description: Ensure all RPC communications between users and nodes use HTTPS or other secure protocols to prevent MitM tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RPC communication is not always securely transported, risking MitM attacks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Configure RPC endpoints to enforce TLS, and inform users to verify connection security.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Network logs and user connection TLS status reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement CSRF and phishing protections for wallet-connection and transaction submission interfaces
checklist_items.description: Develop the dApp frontend with anti-CSRF measures, timeouts, and warnings to prevent session hijacking or phishing-based tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Malicious actors can craft fake interfaces that manipulate user interactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply standard web security best practices like anti-CSRF tokens, domain whitelisting, Content Security Policies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security review reports, penetration test results.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication mechanisms for governance proposal submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only verified and authenticated users can submit governance proposals. This mitigates risks associated with spoofed or unauthorized proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Contract may lack robust verification of the proposer's identity, enabling spoofing or unauthorized proposal creation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement wallet-based authentication, multi-signature requirements, or off-chain identity verification methods.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing verified user identities linked to proposal submissions.

checklist_items.title: Verify whitelisting and active status of Reward Vaults before directing $BGT emissions
checklist_items.description: Prior to processing emissions, the system should check that each Reward Vault is whitelisted and currently active to prevent directing funds to malicious or non-compliant vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator or emission process might direct funds without verifying vault status, leading to fund loss or misallocation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Integrate on-chain whitelist checks and status flags into the emission process.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction records demonstrating whitelist verification steps during emission issuance.

checklist_items.title: Secure validator key management with multi-signature and hardware security modules
checklist_items.description: Protect validator private keys used for signing emission directives and block proposals to prevent spoofing, tampering, or unauthorized actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are susceptible to compromise, enabling attackers to spoof emissions or proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules, multisig signing protocols, and strict operational security procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key access logs, hardware wallet audit reports, and key management policy documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive security audits and formal verification for Reward Vault smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Subject all Reward Vault contracts—especially whitelist, incentive, and administrative functions—to security audits and formal verification to identify and eliminate vulnerabilities that could enable tampering or exploits.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults may contain software vulnerabilities that attackers can exploit to tamper with assets.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable security auditors, apply formal verification tools, and conduct penetration testing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, and test case documentation.

checklist_items.title: Enforce multi-party consensus and multisignature controls for governance proposal approvals and cancellations
checklist_items.description: Require multiple independent signatures or approvals, especially for sensitive operations like proposal cancellations or parameter changes, to prevent unilateral malicious or compromised actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The multisig process for Guardian approvals might be vulnerable to key compromise or inadequate security controls.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multisig with threshold signatures, hardware security modules, and frequent key rotation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig approval logs, threshold settings documentation, and key management records.

checklist_items.title: Require cryptographic signatures for all off-chain RFRV review communications
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all review decisions submitted off-chain are signed with a verifiable cryptographic signature associated with an official identity, preventing spoofing of RFRV approvals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RFRV review communications lack verifiable signatures, enabling impersonation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Integrate digital signatures linked to verified identities or secure signatures from trusted keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed messages, signatures verified against approved public keys.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure communication channels and encryption for off-chain RFRV review discussions
checklist_items.description: Use encrypted and authenticated communication channels (e.g., end-to-end encrypted messaging, secure web portals) for internal BGT Foundation discussions and review decisions to prevent tampering or eavesdropping.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Off-chain discussions are susceptible to interception or tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Adopt secure messaging platforms, enforce access controls, and use encrypted channels.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Communication protocol specifications, encryption certificates, and audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Establish comprehensive monitoring and anomaly detection for smart contract operations
checklist_items.description: Deploy monitoring, alerting, and anomaly detection systems for all critical smart contracts (e.g., Reward Vaults, Governance, Incentive Distributors) to rapidly identify suspicious activities such as unauthorized transactions or unusual gas patterns.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Smart contracts may be exploited without detection if monitoring is insufficient.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement event logging, establish alert thresholds, and integrate with security information and event management (SIEM) systems.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring dashboards, alert logs, incident response documentation.

checklist_items.title: Use hardware wallets and device security best practices for signing critical transactions
checklist_items.description: Require validators, administrators, and users to employ hardware wallets, multi-factor authentication, and secure device practices when signing transactions related to proposal management, token issuance, and key operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Private keys or signing environments are vulnerable to theft or malware, enabling impersonation or tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy hardware wallets, enforce MFA, educate users on security best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature operation logs, key management policies, user attestations.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust access control and role management for smart contracts and off-chain systems
checklist_items.description: Apply the principle of least privilege, role-based access control, and regular permission audits on all systems and smart contracts that manage critical operations, including reward distributions, proposal submissions, and key management.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak access control allows unauthorized privilege escalation or manipulation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use role-based permissions, multi-signature controls, and audit procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs, permissions configuration files, audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Verify that forum submission system enforces strong user authentication, such as wallet-based or on-chain linked identities.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only verified entities can submit RFRVs to prevent spoofing or impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Ensure smart contracts governing $BGT emissions include strict access controls and are audited for privilege escalation vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.description: Prevent malicious actors from exploiting contract flaws to divert or inflate emissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault's logic accepts signed direction transactions without comprehensive privilege checks or audits.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct security audits focusing on access control and privilege escalation vectors in Reward Vault contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports showing secure access controls.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction verification and user confirmations for critical operations such as bridging, staking, and redeeming to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.description: Require users to review all transaction details carefully before signing, especially for actions involving asset transfers.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users do not verify transaction parameters in their wallets before signing, leading to susceptibility to tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encourage user education on verifying transaction details; enforce UI warnings and confirmations in wallets.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User logs showing transaction review steps.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of validators and administrators using hardware security modules (HSMs), multisig, and operational security best practices.
checklist_items.description: Reduce the risk of key compromise that can lead to elevation of privilege or malicious transaction signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator keys are protected by operational security measures; otherwise, they are vulnerable to theft.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules, multisig, regular key rotation, and operational security protocols for key management.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management process documentation and hardware security audits.

checklist_items.title: Establish governance and procedural safeguards, such as multi-party approval for critical actions like setting incentive rates or emergency proposals.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that no single actor can unilaterally perform sensitive operations, reducing risk of malicious activity or privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The current governance process may be vulnerable to collusion or compromised members.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-party approval thresholds, enforce multisig requirements, and conduct regular governance audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed governance approval logs and policy documentation.

checklist_items.title: Verify that all contract addresses used in the protocol are from official sources and have been published and notarized, restricting interaction with unverified contracts.
checklist_items.description: Prevent users from interacting with malicious or spoofed smart contracts during bridging, staking, or other operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users do not verify smart contract addresses before interactions, risking engagement with malicious contracts.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Maintain and publish official contract addresses; utilize external attestations and signed attestations if applicable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Official address list and user verification records.

checklist_items.title: Ensure all web interfaces, including dApps and web portals, are served over HTTPS with strong content security policies and regularly audited for vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.description: Prevent man-in-the-middle and tampering attacks during user interactions such as voting, staking, or redeeming.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User wallet interactions with dApps are susceptible to MitM and tampering if security controls are weak.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS, apply Content Security Policies (CSP), use HSTS headers, and conduct regular vulnerability assessments.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit reports and server configuration documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication for forum users submitting RFRVs
checklist_items.description: Ensure that users submitting RFRV proposals are authenticated via linked on-chain identity or digital signatures to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Require cryptographic signatures on all governance proposals and voting transactions
checklist_items.description: Mandate that all proposals and votes are signed with the submitter's private keys and verify these signatures on-chain to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Contracts do not verify signatures of proposal or vote submissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement signature verification within governance contracts or associated verification services.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain signatures and verification logs.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys used for proposal and vote signing with hardware wallets and multi-factor authentication
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all private keys used by DAO members for governance activities are stored securely using hardware security modules and protected with MFA to prevent unauthorized access.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Members’ private keys are stored in insecure environments or managed improperly.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Adopt hardware wallets, enforce MFA, and conduct regular security audits of key management practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing key storage methods and MFA enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain whitelisting and validation for Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only Reward Vaults verified and approved through on-chain whitelisting mechanisms can receive $BGT emissions, preventing unauthorized or malicious vaults from participating.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault whitelisting process may not exhaustively verify vault legitimacy.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce on-chain whitelist checks before emission transfer, with regular governance updates.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing whitelist validation flows.

checklist_items.title: Conduct security audits and formal verification of the Reward Vault and Bridge contracts
checklist_items.description: Perform comprehensive audits and formal verification of smart contracts involved in reward distribution and asset bridging to detect and fix vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault and Bridge smart contracts may contain exploitable flaws.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable security auditors, conduct formal verification, and implement bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification certificates, and bug bounty submissions.

checklist_items.title: Use secure communication channels and verify transaction details before on-chain signing
checklist_items.description: Encourage users to verify all transaction parameters on their hardware wallets and through trusted interfaces before signing any proposal, transaction, or claim.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User interfaces or wallets may be compromised, leading to tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: User education, use of hardware wallets, verification prompts in wallets.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User confirmation logs, wallet configuration checks.

checklist_items.title: Implement monitoring and alerting for unusual activity in governance proposals and reward distributions
checklist_items.description: Establish real-time monitoring and alerting systems to detect abnormal proposal creation, voting patterns, or reward distribution anomalies, enabling rapid response to potential exploits.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There may be insufficient oversight or detection mechanisms for governance and reward activities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy on-chain analytics, set thresholds for alerts, and maintain incident response procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring dashboards, alert logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication for forum submissions to verify the identity of the RFRV submitter
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only verified users, preferably with on-chain identity linkage, can submit RFRV proposals to prevent impersonation or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain verification of wallet signatures for all governance proposal and vote submissions
checklist_items.description: Require that all proposal and vote submissions are accompanied by cryptographic signatures from verified on-chain wallet addresses to prevent spoofing or repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The on-chain voting and proposal process does not currently enforce signature verification, allowing impersonation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Update proposal and voting smart contracts to verify signatures against known wallet addresses
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing signature verification passes for proposals and votes.

checklist_items.title: Secure the off-chain backend that generates proofs for incentive claims with access controls and regular audits
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the backend service responsible for generating proofs of incentive entitlement is protected against tampering and unauthorized access, as its compromise can lead to fraudulent claims.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The backend service generating proofs for incentive claims could be compromised or have vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust access controls, conduct regular security audits, and monitor for suspicious activities in the backend.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, access logs showing restricted access, and incident response records.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain whitelist verification for Reward Vault addresses before allowing emissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the contract responsible for distributing incentives verifies that the target Reward Vault is whitelisted and active, preventing incentives from being diverted to malicious or unapproved vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system might direct emissions to non-whitelisted or malicious Reward Vaults due to missing on-chain validation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Embed whitelist verification logic into the Reward Vault or emission distribution contract.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit reports verifying whitelist enforcement, on-chain logs confirming whitelist checks during emission events.

checklist_items.title: Secure private key management for Validator nodes and ensure multi-signature controls for critical operations
checklist_items.description: Reduce the risk of elevation of privilege by protecting the validator's signing keys with hardware security modules, multi-signature schemes, and operational security best practices.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's key management environment may be vulnerable to theft or misuse.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets, multi-sig, secure key storage, and operational security protocols.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security policies, key access logs, audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Audit the smart contract code of Reward Vaults and Reward Distributor for security vulnerabilities before deployment
checklist_items.description: Subject all Reward Vault and reward distribution contracts to thorough security audits and formal verification to prevent exploits due to code flaws.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may contain vulnerabilities exploitable by attackers.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: External security audits, formal verification, and bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification certificates, security audit summaries.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure communication channels and verify contract addresses for all bridge interactions
checklist_items.description: Use trusted signer, domain, and communication methods to verify the authenticity of bridge transactions and avoid interactions with malicious counterparties.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users or operators might connect to malicious or impersonating bridge endpoints.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use verified contract addresses, employ cryptographic signatures on off-chain messages, and secure communication protocols.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain logs, verification procedures, audit trails of communication channels.

checklist_items.title: Regularly update and patch the smart contracts and system software involved in staking, redemption, and reward distribution
checklist_items.description: Implement a routine update and patch management process for all relevant smart contracts and interfaces to address identified vulnerabilities promptly.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Vulnerabilities in the system could be exploited if not patched.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish update procedures, bug bounty programs, and continuous security assessment.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Patch deployment records, audit logs, vulnerability reports.

checklist_items.title: Verify that the governance proposal creation process includes multi-signature or guardian approval to prevent unauthorized proposals
checklist_items.description: Ensure governance proposals are only created after multi-party approval, reducing risk of single key compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A vulnerability exists in the governance contract that could allow proposal creation without meeting the required approval process.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-signature approval for proposal creation and deploy rigorous contract audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing multi-signature approval flow.

checklist_items.title: Ensure the identity verification mechanism is in place for RFRV submissions to prevent spoofing by malicious actors
checklist_items.description: Implement cryptographic verification or off-chain identity checks to confirm the submitter's actual identity and prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance forum lacks strong verification mechanisms to authenticate submitters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require cryptographically signed submissions or verified off-chain identities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed messages with verified on-chain identity signatures.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication and access controls for the Reward Vault contract management functions
checklist_items.description: Ensure only authorized and verified entities can perform critical administrative actions on Reward Vaults, preventing privilege escalation and unauthorized changes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control mechanisms may be flawed or insufficient.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use role-based access control with multisignature approval for admin functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports showing access control mechanisms and multisig signatures.

checklist_items.title: Always verify contract addresses and signatures before executing transactions to prevent tampering or phishing attacks
checklist_items.description: Implement wallet and user interface checks to confirm the authenticity of contract addresses and transaction details before signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users cannot reliably distinguish spoofed interfaces from legitimate ones.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use verified bookmarks, wallet warnings, and educate users to verify contract addresses.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of verified transaction details in wallets.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure communication channels (e.g., HTTPS, encrypted RPC) to prevent Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) tampering
checklist_items.description: Require all RPC endpoints and frontend interfaces to use encrypted channels to prevent interception and tampering of data in transit.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Communication with RPC endpoints is not always secured with HTTPS.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS, use VPNs, and secure RPC configurations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration audits showing secure communication enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Configure wallet and dApp interfaces to display all transaction details explicitly and require user confirmation before signing
checklist_items.description: Prevent tampering by ensuring users verify all parameters explicitly and confirm them during transaction signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users do not verify transaction details thoroughly before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Design wallet interfaces to show all transaction details clearly and require manual confirmation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of transaction details shown in wallet confirmation dialogs.

checklist_items.title: Monitor and restrict RPC endpoints' access to prevent malicious or maliciously controlled nodes from capturing user data or conducting MitM attacks
checklist_items.description: Use trusted RPC providers, monitor access, and restrict endpoints to prevent misuse or eavesdropping.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RPC endpoints may be compromised or malicious.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use reputable RPC providers, implement IP restrictions, and monitor traffic for anomalies.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs and monitoring dashboards showing RPC usage patterns.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication mechanisms for BGT Foundation review actions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all review decisions and actions by the BGT Foundation are authenticated using cryptographically verifiable methods to prevent impersonation or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Systems or processes acting on BGT Foundation's review decisions lack robust mechanisms to verify the authenticity of such decisions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., digital signatures, verified identities) for all official communications and review decisions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed approval logs or digital signatures stored securely.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of Validator operators to prevent impersonation and privilege escalation
checklist_items.description: Ensure that private keys used by validators for signing blocks, proposals, and governance actions are stored securely using hardware wallets, multi-signature schemes, and operational security best practices.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A compromise of validator keys can lead to spoofing, tampering, and privilege escalation affecting block proposals, reward distributions, and governance actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules, multi-signature schemes, and operational security practices for key storage.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audit reports, key access logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify the whitelisting status of Reward Vault contracts before emission transactions
checklist_items.description: Implement on-chain checks to ensure that $BGT emissions are directed only to trusted and whitelisted Reward Vaults, preventing the diversion of funds to malicious or unapproved contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A validator might maliciously or accidentally direct emissions to non-whitelisted or faulty vaults; the system lacks rigorous on-chain verification of vault whitelist status.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce whitelist checks on-chain before processing emission transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain transaction logs showing whitelist status verification.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive security audits and formal verification for Reward Vault smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Subject Reward Vault contracts to rigorous security audits and formal verification processes to identify and remediate vulnerabilities that could be exploited for tampering, DoS, or privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may contain inherent software vulnerabilities exploitable by attackers.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable security auditors, perform formal verification, and establish bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, bug bounty submission records.

checklist_items.title: It's crucial to secure off-chain oracle and proof generation services used in incentive distribution
checklist_items.description: Ensure the off-chain backend services generating proofs for incentive claims are secured against tampering, with strong access controls, regular audits, and transparency measures.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The off-chain proof generation service could be compromised, leading to fraudulent or invalid incentive claims.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure infrastructure, use cryptographic proofs, and establish verifiable audit logs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs, third-party security assessments, cryptographic proof verification procedures.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication linked to on-chain identity for RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Verify that only users with verified on-chain identities can submit RFRVs to prevent impersonation and spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain verification of Reward Vault whitelist status before processing emissions
checklist_items.description: Configure the Reward Distributor Contract to verify the Reward Vault's whitelisted status on-chain prior to transferring $BGT emissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system (e.g., Distributor Contract 214) might not robustly check the status/validity of a target Reward Vault before attempting to send emissions, or this check could be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Strict on-chain verification that target Reward Vaults are whitelisted and active before emissions are processed. Clear UI for validators showing vault status. Monitoring for emissions to problematic or non-whitelisted vaults.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain transaction logs confirming whitelist status check before emission transfer.

checklist_items.title: Secure validator keys used for signing reward emission transactions
checklist_items.description: Require validators to use hardware security modules or multisignature wallets to protect private keys used for signing and directing $BGT emissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Validator's private key used for signing emission direction transactions can be compromised, allowing an attacker to spoof $BGT emission directives.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure validator key management (e.g., HSMs, robust operational security). Optional: Implement a cooldown period or multi-signature requirement for changing emission directions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit of private key management procedures and hardware setups.

checklist_items.title: Implement rigorous input validation for smart contract functions handling proposals and votes
checklist_items.description: Smart contracts governing governance proposals and votes must validate all input data to prevent tampering and malicious payloads.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A malicious governance proposal or vote payload can be crafted to manipulate the contract logic if input validation is weak.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use of validated, standard libraries for input validation; thorough code review and formal verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit reports showing validation mechanisms.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure, verifiable off-chain data protocols for incentive proof generation
checklist_items.description: The off-chain backend generating proofs for incentives must be secured against tampering, with cryptographic proof validation embedded on-chain.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The backend service generating proofs for BGTIncentiveDistributor is compromised or has vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure the backend proof generation service, implement strong validation of proofs within the smart contract, ensure transparency and auditability of the proof generation process, consider decentralized proof mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of proof generation, cryptographic proof validation code audits.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong, verifyable authentication for the Governance Forum to confirm submitters' identities
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all RFRV submitters are verified through robust on-chain or off-chain identity mechanisms to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Systems or processes acting on BGT Foundation's review decisions lack robust mechanisms to verify the authenticity of such decisions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., digital signatures, verified identities) for all official communications and actions taken by the BGT Foundation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing verified identities and signatures at proposal submission.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of Validators using hardware security modules and multi-signature controls
checklist_items.description: To prevent privilege escalation, enforce the use of hardware wallets and multisig operations for Validator private keys used in critical consensus and emission signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's key is a critical signing credential that can be exploited if compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce hardware wallet usage, multi-signature governance for key operations, and regular key security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration records showing hardware wallets and multisig addresses are used for Validator keys.

checklist_items.title: Validate on-chain Reward Vault whitelist status before directing $BGT emissions
checklist_items.description: Implement on-chain checks to ensure emission directions are only sent to Reward Vaults that pass whitelisting and validity verification.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator can direct emissions arbitrarily if checks are flawed or inadequate.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Integrate whitelist verification logic into emission direction functions with on-chain enforcement.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit trail of emission transactions and whitelist check logs.

checklist_items.title: Monitor and enforce minimum stake requirements for validator qualification in smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the consensus and staking smart contracts implement and verify minimum stake thresholds to prevent unauthorized validator qualification.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator can exploit flawed validation logic to become a validator without meeting minimum stake requirements.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strict minimum stake enforcement and regular audits of validator qualification procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit reports confirming enforcement of minimum stake requirements.

checklist_items.title: Use secure, verified RPC endpoints and verify their authenticity before connection
checklist_items.description: Require users to connect only to RPC providers with verified, trusted certificates and implement warnings for unknown or untrusted endpoints.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User connects to a malicious or logging RPC endpoint that can perform MitM or data exfiltration.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce using only RPC URLs from trusted sources, and browser or wallet warnings about unverified endpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration logs showing only approved RPC URLs are used.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain transaction logs and non-repudiable proof mechanisms for all governance proposals and actions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all proposals, votes, and governance decisions are recorded on-chain with cryptographic proof to prevent actors from denying their actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Actions on a public blockchain are inherently non-repudiable due to transaction history.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Design governance actions to emit signed events stored on-chain or use multisig approval signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Blockchain transaction records with signatures and timestamps.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication linking forum submissions to on-chain identities
checklist_items.description: Verify if the Governance Forum requires users to authenticate via their on-chain wallets for RFRV submissions to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Restrict access to confidential RFRV information using secure storage and access controls
checklist_items.description: Ensure that detailed RFRV and contributor information is stored securely off-chain with strict access controls to prevent unintended disclosure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The exact vesting details for each contributor may be stored on-chain or off-chain, and such information might be unintentionally exposed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Minimize on-chain storage of sensitive details; secure off-chain data storage with strict access controls.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control logs showing restricted access to sensitive data.

checklist_items.title: Verify whitelist status before directing rewards or token transfers to Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Check that the reward distribution system enforces strict whitelist verification of Reward Vaults before directing emissions to prevent misallocation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator or reward distributor might direct emissions to non-whitelisted or malicious vaults if proper checks are absent.
