checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain whitelist verification of Reward Vaults before emission transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports confirming whitelist enforcement in reward distribution logic.

checklist_items.title: Secure private key management for Validator nodes to prevent emission spoofing and privilege escalation
checklist_items.description: Ensure that Validator private keys controlling reward emissions are stored and operated within secure hardware modules with multi-party signing where applicable.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator's private keys can be compromised if stored insecurely, leading to emission diversion.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules (HSMs) and multisignature schemes for key operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Certificate of hardware module deployment and key access logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement multisig and role-based access controls for Reward Vaults to prevent unauthorized administrative actions
checklist_items.description: Configure Reward Vault smart contracts to require multisig approval and role-based permissions for administrative functions such as setting parameters or withdrawing funds.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault admin functions might have flawed access restrictions, allowing privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply role-based access control with multisignature approval on all administrative functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports showing role restrictions and multisig enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit and verify reward distribution and incentive calculations in Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Ensure that Reward Vault smart contracts are audited for correct and fair incentive calculations, and implement on-chain checks where possible.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault's incentive logic might be flawed, allowing reward theft or misallocations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Conduct formal verification and regular audits of incentive calculation code.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit certificates and formal verification reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication linking forum accounts to on-chain identities
checklist_items.description: Verify that forum users submitting RFRVs are authenticated through on-chain identity mechanisms to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Conduct regular security audits and formal verification of reward distribution smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure Reward Vaults and incentive distribution contracts are audited for vulnerabilities in reward calculation and distribution logic to prevent exploitation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault and incentive distribution contracts may contain vulnerabilities exploitable for unfair reward claims.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Perform comprehensive security audits and formal verification of reward contracts.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification proofs, and test results demonstrating contract security.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-signature authorization for critical governance proposal actions
checklist_items.description: Implement multi-signature or multi-party approval mechanisms for governance proposals and cancellations to prevent unilateral malicious actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance proposals can be maliciously created or canceled without multi-party approval.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce multisig or multi-party approvals for proposal creation and cancellation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Configuration documentation showing multisig requirements, signed approval records.

checklist_items.title: Implement strict address and contract verification procedures for all token transactions
checklist_items.description: Require users and the system to verify the authenticity of contract addresses and transaction parameters before signing or execution to prevent spoofed interactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may be tricked into interacting with malicious or spoofed contracts or interfaces.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce verification of contract addresses, signatures, and transaction details before signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of user verification steps, transaction records confirming address validation.

checklist_items.title: Secure private key management for validators and key holders
checklist_items.description: Use hardware wallets, multi-signature schemes, and operational security best practices to protect private keys controlling validator actions and emission directions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are vulnerable to theft or compromise, enabling spoofing or unauthorized actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy hardware security modules, enforce operational security protocols, and enforce multi-signature control over key actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies, hardware wallet deployment records, access logs.

checklist_items.title: Use secure communication channels and verify RPC endpoint authenticity
checklist_items.description: Ensure RPC endpoints are accessed over HTTPS, verified through cryptographic means, and users are educated to avoid connecting to untrusted endpoints.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RPC endpoints may be malicious or untrusted, leading to MitM and data tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use only official, verified RPC URLs; enforce HTTPS; educate users to verify endpoint authenticity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Communication logs, endpoint verification procedures, wallet settings reviews.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strong cryptographic authentication for RFRV submissions to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.description: Implement cryptographic verification methods (e.g., wallet signatures) to ensure the submitter's identity in the Governance Forum matches their on-chain identity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement cryptographic signature verification for all forum submissions against registered on-chain identities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing verified signatures linked to registered identities.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain verification of proposal authorship and submissions to establish non-repudiable attribution.
checklist_items.description: Require that RFRV submissions or governance proposals are accompanied by on-chain signatures or proof of authorship to prevent actors from denying their submissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The process for recording and finalizing review decisions by the BGT Foundation does not create a tamper-proof, attributable record of who made the decision and when.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposers to include signed statements or on-chain attestations as part of RFRV submissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain transaction records with valid signatures from the purported submitter.

checklist_items.title: Secure off-chain communication channels among Guardians to prevent premature or leaked cancellation intents.
checklist_items.description: Establish and enforce the use of secure, confidential communication protocols (e.g., encrypted messaging, multi-party secure channels) for Guardians coordinating proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Guardians coordinate or discuss potential cancellations off-chain before executing the multisig transaction.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use encrypted messaging platforms, enforce operational security policies, and conduct regular security training for Guardians.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs of Guardian communications within secure channels.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-sig threshold validation and regular audits for the Guardian multisig infrastructure.
checklist_items.description: Apply rigorous multi-party signature verification with secure, audited multisignature contracts to ensure proposal cancellations require consensus (e.g., >=5 out of 9 signatures).
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Timelock Contract's multisig verification logic for proposal cancellations could be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use established, well-audited multisig libraries; conduct formal verification and routine audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports showing multisig verification correctness.

checklist_items.title: Strengthen access control and role management in Reward Vaults to prevent unauthorized operations.
checklist_items.description: Implement role-based access controls with least privilege principles for all Reward Vault administrative functions, ensuring only authorized, multi-signed, or time-locked actions are permissible.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for functions like adding incentives or setting rates is flawed or bypassable.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce strict role restrictions, multi-sig for admin actions, and regular access audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs, smart contract source code review reports.

checklist_items.title: Design reward distribution mechanisms with built-in safeguards against manipulation and exploits.
checklist_items.description: Develop and audit reward calculation and distribution smart contracts to prevent over-claiming and ensure fair payoff based on verified, tamper-resistant data inputs.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A flaw in the reward calculation or distribution logic could be exploited to unfairly claim incentives.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Apply formal verification, implement threshold and limit checks, and conduct rigorous audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, on-chain reward logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify implementation of strong authentication mechanisms for the Governance Forum (Component 223)
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only verified and authenticated users, such as those with on-chain identity, can submit RFRV proposals to prevent impersonation or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., link forum accounts to verified on-chain identities, multi-factor authentication)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing user verification methods at proposal submission.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain whitelisting checks for Reward Vaults (Component 209) before directing emissions or assets
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all Reward Vaults receiving $BGT emissions are properly whitelisted and active, reducing the risk of directing funds to malicious or faulty vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system (e.g., Distributor Contract 214) might not robustly check vault whitelisting status before emissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce on-chain verification that target Reward Vaults are whitelisted and active before allowing $BGT emissions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs and transaction records showing whitelisting checks during emission transactions.

checklist_items.title: Secure private key management practices for Validator nodes (Actor 2) and implement multi-signature controls for critical operations
checklist_items.description: Ensure that Validator keys used for proposing blocks, signing emissions, or executing admin functions are stored in secure hardware devices and protected by multi-signature schemes to prevent unauthorized control or impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The validator's private keys can be compromised, allowing impersonation or privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware security modules (HSMs), multi-signature schemes, and operational security best practices for validator key storage.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audit logs, hardware security module access logs.

checklist_items.title: Conduct regular security audits and formal verification of Reward Vault and Bridge contracts including their access controls and core logic
checklist_items.description: Regular audits and formal verification can identify and remediate vulnerabilities that could be exploited for tampering, unauthorized privilege escalation, or DoS attacks in smart contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults and Bridge contracts may contain exploitable flaws.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable security auditors and formal verification tools for contract code.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and formal verification documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain validation for proposal creation thresholds and voting power checks within Governance Contracts (Component 212)
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the logic enforcing proposal thresholds (e.g., 10,000 $BGT) and voting power calculations are correctly implemented and resistant to exploit, preventing unauthorized proposal creation and vote manipulation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The contract may have a flaw in its threshold and power calculation mechanisms.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Review and verify on-chain threshold enforcement and snapshot logic with formal methods.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Code review reports, on-chain data showing threshold enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Use secure communication channels and verify domain authenticity for all user-facing interfaces such as BeraHub, HoneySwap, and governance sites
checklist_items.description: Users should access dApps only through official, verified URLs and enable security features like HTTPS, HSTS, and domain verification to prevent spoofing and man-in-the-middle attacks during critical operations like staking, swapping, and voting.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users cannot reliably distinguish legitimate dApp interfaces from malicious spoofed versions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce the use of official URLs, implement domain monitoring, educate users on verifying site authenticity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User reports, domain registration records, browser security warnings.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication for BGT Foundation review decisions
checklist_items.description: Ensure all review decisions are authenticated via cryptographic signatures or verified identity mechanisms to prevent impersonation or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Systems or processes acting on BGT Foundation's review decisions lack robust mechanisms to verify the authenticity of such decisions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., digital signatures, verified identity protocols)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed review decision records with cryptographic verification.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys and operational procedures for Validator nodes
checklist_items.description: Adopt multi-factor authentication, hardware security modules, and operational security best practices to prevent private key compromise used for block proposal and reward signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Validator's block signing key can be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure validator key management (e.g., HSMs, multi-sig, hardware wallets), regular key rotation, and operational security protocols.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing key management procedures and hardware wallet usage.

checklist_items.title: Verify whitelisted and active Reward Vaults before directing emissions
checklist_items.description: Implement on-chain checks to ensure that only Reward Vaults that are whitelisted and active receive $BGT emissions, preventing diversion to malicious or unverified vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator can maliciously or accidentally direct emissions to non-whitelisted or faulty vaults.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: On-chain verification of vault whitelist status prior to emission transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Log of emission transactions with verification check results.

checklist_items.title: Enforce formal security audits and formal verification of smart contracts
checklist_items.description: All Reward Vaults, Reward Distributor, and related contracts must undergo rigorous security audits and formal verification to identify and fix vulnerabilities before deployment.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts contain exploitable flaws that could be exploited during deployment.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable security firms for audits, formal verification, and implement bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, and bounty program reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain verifiable audit trails for governance and review actions
checklist_items.description: Ensure all proposals, decisions, and review actions are recorded in an immutable and verifiable on-chain log, possibly with multi-party signatures, to prevent repudiation or denial.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The process for recording and finalizing review decisions by the BGT Foundation does not create a tamper-proof, attributable record.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multi-signature on-chain signatures or cryptographically attested logs for governance decisions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain signed records of decisions or audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Deploy official verified smart contract addresses and disseminate to users
checklist_items.description: Publish and maintain a registry of officially verified smart contract addresses for bridges, vaults, and other critical assets, guiding users to avoid interacting with malicious or spoofed contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Attackers can deploy malicious smart contracts on Berachain that impersonate legitimate Protocol contracts.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Publicly announce and update a registry of trusted contracts, enforce address whitelists within dApps and wallets.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Public registry online, references in wallet or dApp interfaces.

checklist_items.title: Use https and verify TLS certificate for RPC endpoints
checklist_items.description: Ensure wallets and users verify the RPC endpoints they connect to use HTTPS with valid TLS certificates, preventing Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attacks when connecting to Berachain nodes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Attacker can perform a Man-in-the-Middle (MitM) attack to intercept and tamper RPC communication.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS with valid TLS certificates for RPC services. Educate users to verify the connection security.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Wallet configuration screenshots showing HTTPS endpoints, TLS certificate validation logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong on-chain identity verification for forum submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that users submitting RFRV proposals are verified through their on-chain identities to prevent impersonation and spoofing attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Enforce minimum stake requirements in the validator onboarding process
checklist_items.description: Verify that only validators meeting the minimum staking threshold can participate in consensus to prevent privilege escalation through stake manipulation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The staking logic in Berachain (201) might have flaws in enforcing minimum staking amounts or other validator criteria.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Strict on-chain enforcement of minimum staking amounts and other validator qualification criteria. Regular security audits of the staking logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports demonstrating enforcement of stake thresholds.

checklist_items.title: Secure validator key management to prevent signing with compromised keys
checklist_items.description: Ensure private keys used by validators are stored in hardware security modules (HSMs) or multi-signature setups to prevent elevation of privilege through key compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Validator's private key(s) used for signing emission direction transactions can be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure hardware wallets, HSMs, and multisig for validator key storage. Enforce operational security best practices.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Deployment records showing hardware vaults or multisig account configurations.

checklist_items.title: Implement robust access control and role management in Reward Vault contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only authorized roles (e.g., Token Manager) can perform sensitive functions like adding incentive tokens or setting incentive rates.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for functions like adding incentives or setting rates is flawed or bypassable.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement and audit role-based access control mechanisms such as multi-signature requirements or role-based permissions. Follow best practices in smart contract security.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract audit reports verifying access controls.

checklist_items.title: Use secure private key management practices for all critical signing keys
checklist_items.description: All private keys used for signing critical transactions (e.g., emission directions, reward distribution, proposal signatures) must be stored securely with hardware security modules or multi-signature schemes to prevent unauthorized privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A Validator's private key used for signing emission direction transactions can be compromised.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Securing at-rest keys with hardware modules, implementing multisig signing protocols.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Deployment and operational records showing hardware wallet or multisig setup.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive audit and formal verification of Reward Vault and Governance contracts
checklist_items.description: All smart contracts involved in reward distribution, governance, and voting should undergo security audits and formal verification to identify and fix vulnerabilities that could be exploited for tampering or privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The reward and governance smart contracts might contain vulnerabilities exploitable for privilege escalation or tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable security auditors and pursue formal verification of smart contract code.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit certificates and verification reports.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict verification of target vault status before emission transfers
checklist_items.description: Prior to directing $BGT emissions to Reward Vaults, perform on-chain checks to confirm that the vault is whitelisted, active, and capable of accepting emissions, to prevent sending to malicious or non-functional vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator can choose to maliciously or accidentally direct emissions to non-whitelisted or faulty vaults.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain verification of vault status, integrated into reward emission logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing verification steps and whitelisted vaults.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong on-chain identity verification for RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Governance Forum requires submitters to authenticate using their wallet addresses linked to verified on-chain identities to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Require cryptographic signatures for governance proposal submissions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that all governance proposals are signed with the submitter's private key to establish non-repudiation and prevent malicious submission with forged identities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak attribution on the forum could allow a protocol to disown a controversial or failed RFRV submission.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encourage or enforce signed proposals with cryptographic signatures from verified keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed message with associated verification process logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement access controls and audit logs for RFRV post modifications
checklist_items.description: Set up strict access controls for the Governance Forum to prevent unauthorized editing of RFRV submissions and maintain tamper-evident audit logs of all changes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum may allow post editing by compromised accounts, and audit trails might be insufficient.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce strict access controls and maintain verifiable audit logs for all RFRV modifications.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs with timestamps and account identifiers of all post edits.

checklist_items.title: Configure smart contracts to verify whitelist status before processing emissions or rewards
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the Reward Vaults and Distributor contracts implement on-chain checks to verify that emission targets are whitelisted and active before executing transfer or reward distribution functions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator or actor can direct emissions to non-whitelisted or malicious vaults due to insufficient on-chain validation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain whitelist verification within emission and reward distribution functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract code review and transaction logs showing whitelist checks during emission execution.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of guardian multisig signers with hardware security modules or multi-factor authentication
checklist_items.description: Implement hardware security modules (HSMs) and require multi-factor authentication for Guardian multisig signers to prevent unauthorized proposal cancellations via compromised keys.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Compromise of guardian private keys could lead to unauthorized proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use HSMs, secure key storage, and multi-factor authentication for multisig signers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit reports verifying key management controls, MFA logs.

checklist_items.title: Establish robust security practices for off-chain guardian communication channels
checklist_items.description: Use encrypted, authenticated, and trusted communication channels (e.g., secure messaging, VPNs) for guardians when coordinating proposal cancellations or governance decisions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Off-chain communication channels may be insecure or susceptible to leaks or tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use end-to-end encrypted and verified communication channels for guardian coordination.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Operational procedures documenting secure communication protocols.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain validation of Guardian multisig signatures for proposal cancellations
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the multisig contract enforces the 5-of-9 signature requirement correctly and has been formally verified to prevent signature aggregation flaws.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Multisig verification logic may contain bugs allowing fewer signatures to authorize cancellations.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use formally verified multisig libraries and conduct rigorous audits of multisig logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification proofs, signature verification process logs.

checklist_items.title: Zone private keys of validator operators with hardware security modules and enforce strict access controls
checklist_items.description: Require validators to manage their signing keys within hardware security modules (HSMs) and enforce strict access controls to prevent key theft or misuse that could lead to fraudulent emissions or proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys are vulnerable to theft or misuse, enabling spoofing or unauthorized emissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Mandate HSMs, multi-factor access, and operational security procedures for validator keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit reports of key management practices.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication for BGT Foundation review actions
checklist_items.description: Require cryptographic signatures and verification that review submissions originate from verified Foundation members.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Systems lack robust mechanisms to verify the authenticity of review decisions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use cryptographic signatures and secure identity verification methods for Foundation members' review actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs showing signed review decisions with cryptographic proof.

checklist_items.title: Restrict on-chain storage of detailed personal and allocation information
checklist_items.description: Minimize storage of confidential contributor data on-chain to reduce risk of unintentional disclosure.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: On-chain storage of detailed allocation and vesting schedule details may lead to unintentional disclosure.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use aggregated data, Merkle proofs, or off-chain privacy-preserving techniques for sensitive contributor information.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports on storage design and data access controls.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-signature controls for administrative functions in Reward Vault contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure all critical administrative actions (e.g., setting incentive rates, pausing contracts) require multi-party approvals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts may have flawed access control mechanisms.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multisig with secure key management for admin functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of admin actions signed by multiple authorized parties.

checklist_items.title: Validate all contract addresses and whitelists before processing emissions or reward distributions
checklist_items.description: Implement on-chain verification to ensure rewards are directed only to validated and whitelisted Reward Vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults may be directed to non-whitelisted or malicious contracts if validation is weak.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce strict whitelist checks on-chain before emission or reward distribution.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs and smart contract audit reports verifying whitelist enforcement.

checklist_items.title: Enhance private key security for validators and Foundation members
checklist_items.description: Use hardware security modules (HSMs) or multi-signature schemes to protect private keys used for signing critical transactions such as emission directives and governance actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator and Foundation private keys are vulnerable to theft or compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement hardware security modules and multi-signature schemes for all critical keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security audit reports and key management policies.

checklist_items.title: Use off-chain secure channels with encryption for Guardians' communication during governance review and proposal cancellation processes
checklist_items.description: Ensure Guardians' off-chain communications regarding proposal cancellations are encrypted and access-controlled to prevent information leakage and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Guardians coordinate or discuss proposals off-chain, risking information leakage.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Establish encrypted, authenticated channels (e.g., Signal, PGP) for Guardians' off-chain discussions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Communication protocol audit documentation, logs of Guardians' communications.

checklist_items.title: Implement multisignature scheme and regular audits for proposal submission and execution interfaces
checklist_items.description: Require multi-party signatures for proposal submissions and execution triggers to prevent single point of compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak access controls or single signatory proposals might allow impersonation or denial.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multisig signing schemes and routine security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature logs, audit reports, multisig setup documentation.

checklist_items.title: Verify signing environment and transaction details before execution
checklist_items.description: Require Validator or user to review and confirm all transaction parameters in a secure environment (e.g., hardware wallet) prior to signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User or Validator might sign altered transactions due to malware or interface tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Promote hardware wallet usage and user education for transaction verification.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training protocols, wallet security policies.

checklist_items.title: Utilize hardware wallets and MFA for critical operations involving reward distributions, emissions, and governance actions
checklist_items.description: Enhance private key security by using hardware wallets and multi-factor authentication for sensitive signing activities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Private keys are susceptible to theft or unauthorized use.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require hardware wallets and MFA for all critical signatures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security policies, hardware wallet usage logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify that forum submission processes require strong authentication tied to on-chain identity.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that entities submitting RFRV proposals are authenticated through mechanisms such as wallet-based login or cryptographic signatures linked to verified on-chain identities, aligning with assumptions that the Governance Forum lacks strong verification.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum (223) does not have robust verification mechanisms for submitters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement cryptographic verification linked to on-chain identities or multi-factor authentication methods.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing verification steps for RFRV submitters.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain validation for Reward Vault whitelisting status before processing emissions.
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the system verifies the whitelist status and validity of Reward Vaults (209) on-chain before directing $BGT emissions, mitigating risks associated with directing funds to unverified or malicious vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system may not enforce strict whitelist checks for emission targets.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Add on-chain whitelist validation logic in the emission process, enforce checks prior to fund transfers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction records showing whitelist validation steps.

checklist_items.title: Conduct comprehensive security audits and formal verification of Reward Vault smart contracts before deployment.
checklist_items.description: Since vulnerabilities in Reward Vaults (209) can lead to high-impact exploits, perform rigorous audits and formal verification of their code, especially focusing on authorization and reward distribution logic.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault contracts are developed by third parties or custom code with potential vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable auditors and incorporate formal verification tools into the development process.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and verification test results.

checklist_items.title: Ensure transaction signing occurs within secure environments such as hardware wallets.
checklist_items.description: To prevent tampering and ensure integrity during token transfer, users should perform transaction signing using hardware wallets or trusted devices, mitigating risks of client-side tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User endpoints or wallets are vulnerable to malware or client-side tampering.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Promote hardware wallet usage, educate users on verifying transaction details.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User verification logs or wallet signatures confirming transaction details.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure communication protocols (HTTPS, SSL/TLS) for all RPC endpoints and wallet interactions.
checklist_items.description: Prevent man-in-the-middle attacks by enforcing the use of HTTPS with valid certificates for RPC endpoints and ensuring wallet interactions occur over secure channels.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Communication between user wallets and RPC endpoints is vulnerable to MitM attacks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Configure all RPC endpoints to enforce HTTPS, monitor for certificate validity, and educate users to verify endpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security configuration audits, certificate validation reports.

checklist_items.title: Maintain comprehensive audit logs and use cryptographically verifiable signatures for all governance proposal submissions and actions.
checklist_items.description: Ensure every proposal, vote, or governance action is recorded with immutable, verifiable signatures or blockchain events to prevent repudiation and enable accountability.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance system may lack tamper-proof record-keeping for proposal and voting activities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain logging with digital signatures or multisig approvals for proposals and votes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed proposal and vote data, blockchain audit logs.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access controls and multi-factor authentication for wallets and accounts used for governance and administrative actions.
checklist_items.description: Reduce the risk of privilege escalation or unauthorized actions by implementing MFA and role-based access controls for all accounts involved in proposal creation, voting, or admin functions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Access to governance and admin functions is vulnerable due to weak controls.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy MFA, least privilege policies, and conduct regular access audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access logs, MFA activation records.

checklist_items.title: Verify contract addresses are correct and match official sources before interaction.
checklist_items.description: Implement client-side or UI controls to ensure that users verify the smart contract addresses of Vaults, Bridge contracts, or other critical components to prevent interaction with spoofed contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may interact with malicious or fake smart contracts due to lack of address verification.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use verified contract address lists, display known addresses prominently, and educate users.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshots of transaction confirmations, address verification steps.

checklist_items.title: Configure wallets and dApps to clearly display transaction data and confirm details before signing.
checklist_items.description: Enhance user verification by ensuring that wallet interfaces prominently display all transaction details, including amounts, recipient addresses, and contract interactions, to prevent tampering or misrepresentation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users often sign transactions without verifying details.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: User education campaigns, wallet UI improvements to show details clearly.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User confirmation logs, UI design reviews.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication linking forum submissions to verified on-chain identities
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only verified on-chain identities can submit RFRV proposals to prevent impersonation or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Enforce cryptographic signatures on RFRV submissions to ensure authenticity and non-repudiation
checklist_items.description: Require that all proposals for whitelisting are signed with an official private key and verifiable on-chain, preventing spoofing and repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system does not currently verify signatures attached to RFRV submissions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require cryptographic signatures from authorized key holders for all RFRV proposals.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Verification logs showing signatures attached and validated at submission.

checklist_items.title: Verify that the connection between user wallets and the dApp interface is secure via HTTPS with strict TLS enforcement
checklist_items.description: Prevent MitM attacks and tampering during wallet interactions by ensuring all communications are encrypted and the domain is verified.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RPC endpoint (Component 220) is not currently enforced with strict transport layer security.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require HTTPS with valid certificates, implement HSTS, and educate users to verify the website URL.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Certificate validation logs, browser security reports.

checklist_items.title: Ensure all smart contract code undergoes formal security audits and uses well-established libraries
checklist_items.description: Mitigate tampering and exploitation risks in reward vaults, bridge contracts, and other critical components by conducting formal verification and code audits.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults (Component 201) may contain vulnerabilities if not properly vetted.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require formal audits and use of verified libraries for all smart contract deployments.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, verified library deployment records.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain whitelisting with rigorous checks for Reward Vaults and other critical assets
checklist_items.description: Prevent directing incentives or assets to malicious or non-whitelisted vaults by enforcing on-chain whitelist verification before transfer or emission.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator or operator may direct to non-whitelisted or malicious vaults.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require whitelist verification on-chain before emissions or transfers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing whitelist checks and approvals.

checklist_items.title: Establish multi-party approval mechanisms for sensitive governance proposals and administrative actions
checklist_items.description: Require multiple independent approvals or guardians' consensus before executing high-impact proposals or parameter changes to prevent tampering or unilateral privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Governance processes may lack multi-party approval, enabling single actor malicious actions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement multi-signature or consensus-based approval workflows.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Records of multi-party approvals, on-chain signatures.

checklist_items.title: Use secure hardware wallets and verify all transaction parameters before signing
checklist_items.description: Protect against tampering during transaction signing by ensuring the device is uncompromised and transaction details are explicitly verified by the user.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users rely on third-party interfaces and may be vulnerable to malware or phishing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require hardware wallets, educate users to verify all transaction details before signing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User confirmation logs, hardware wallet event logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement rate limiting and monitoring on RPC endpoints to prevent spoofing and denial of service attacks
checklist_items.description: Mitigate impersonation and DoS risks originating from malicious RPC endpoints or network attacks by restricting request rates and monitoring abnormal activity.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RPC endpoints may be vulnerable to abuse or traffic floods.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy rate limiting, anomaly detection, and alerting on RPC services.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Monitoring dashboards, incident logs.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys with hardware security modules (HSMs) or multisignature arrangements
checklist_items.description: Prevent privilege escalation or unauthorized control over critical functions by storing keys in secure hardware modules or requiring multiple approvals for key usage.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Keys controlling Reward Vault admin functions may be vulnerable to theft or misuse.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use HSMs or multisignature key management for all administrative keys.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies, key access logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-party signatures and threshold controls for proposal cancellations and critical governance actions
checklist_items.description: Require a minimum number of guardians' signatures before executing proposal cancellations or sensitive changes, reducing risk of malicious or unilateral operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Proposal cancellations may be initiated by guardians or external parties with insufficient safeguards.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Set multi-signature or threshold signature requirements for governance actions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Consensus records, signed approval logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication for forum RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Governance Forum requires wallet-based authentication or verified identity for users submitting RFRVs to prevent spoofing and impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain verification for governance proposal creation
checklist_items.description: Verify that governance proposals are validated against on-chain token balances or delegated voting power to prevent proposals from being created without sufficient $BGT holdings.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: There is a vulnerability in the Governance Contracts (212) related to determining voting power (e.g., from $BGT balance/delegation at snapshot) or vote counting.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits of Governance Contracts, formal verification of vote counting and power calculation logic, use of secure and well-tested snapshot mechanisms.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain proof of vault balances and proposal records.

checklist_items.title: Implement multisig and timelock controls for proposal cancellations
checklist_items.description: Ensure that proposal cancellation actions require multi-signature approval from multiple Guardians with cryptographic evidence, and enforce timelock periods to prevent rapid arbitrary cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Timelock Contract might incorrectly validate a cancellation signal if enough Guardian keys are compromised and used to spoof a legitimate multisig action.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement robust private key security measures for Guardians, ensure multisig validation is correct, and use secure multisig libraries.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig transaction signatures, audit logs of Guardian approvals.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure communication channels and off-chain verification for RFRV review outcomes
checklist_items.description: Use cryptographically signed messages or verified on-chain commitments to confirm review decisions made by the BGT Foundation, preventing spoofing or repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The process for recording and finalizing review decisions by the BGT Foundation does not create a tamper-proof, attributable record of who made the decision and when.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Maintain secure, immutable, and attributable audit trails of all RFRV review activities and final decisions, possibly using multi-party signatures or on-chain logs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed review reports, on-chain event logs of decisions.

checklist_items.title: Verify whitelist status of Reward Vaults before directing emissions
checklist_items.description: Implement on-chain checks to ensure that only whitelisted and active Reward Vaults can receive $BGT emissions to prevent directing funds to malicious or unapproved contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The system (e.g., Distributor Contract 214) might not robustly check the status/validity of a target Reward Vault before attempting to send emissions, or this check could be flawed.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: On-chain verification of vault whitelist and active status prior to emission transfer, with multi-sig approval for whitelist updates.
checklist_items.evidence_required: On-chain records of whitelist status, logs of emission transactions.

checklist_items.title: Implement secure key management and limit administrative privileges on Reward Vaults
checklist_items.description: Use hardware security modules, multisignature controls, and minimum necessary privileges to protect administrative functions of Reward Vaults against elevation of privilege attacks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The administrative controls for the Reward Vault are compromised (e.g., private key theft) or contain vulnerabilities, leading to unauthorized control.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Secure admin keys with hardware wallets, enforce multisig policies, apply timelocks, audit admin controls.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Private key storage policies, multisig transaction logs, audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Deploy contracts using security best practices and conduct audits
checklist_items.description: Prior to deploying Reward Vaults or other critical contracts, conduct comprehensive audits and formal verification to identify and eliminate vulnerabilities that could be exploited for tampering or DoS.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault smart contract deployed by the Protocol contains exploitable flaws, such as reentrancy or integer overflows.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require audits by reputable security firms, use established libraries, perform formal verification where feasible.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification proofs.

checklist_items.title: Enforce strict access control and role separation for governance and operational functions
checklist_items.description: Design governance and operational smart contracts with role-based access controls, avoiding overprivileged accounts, and including multi-signature or multisig approval for critical actions such as proposal creation, proposal execution, or param changes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Weak access controls or misconfigured privileges in governance contracts could allow unauthorized proposal creation, alteration, or execution.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement role-based access controls, multi-signature approvals, and audit logs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control configuration, multisig transaction logs.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys and use hardware signing modules for critical actions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that private keys used by validators, Guardians, governance members, and other privileged actors are stored in hardware security modules (HSMs) or multisig wallets, and implement operational security best practices to prevent compromise.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Private keys associated with critical roles can be compromised through phishing, malware, or poor operational security.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use hardware wallets or HSMs, enforce multi-factor authentication, regular key rotation, and security reviews.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies, key usage logs, review reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication for RFRV submissions
checklist_items.description: Require submitters of RFRV proposals to authenticate via verified on-chain identity or secure login methods to prevent impersonation or spoofing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum (Component 223) lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing user identity verification process for RFRV submissions.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain verification of governance proposal authenticity
checklist_items.description: Ensure that governance proposals are accompanied by cryptographic signatures from authorized signers, enabling verification of origin and preventing tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance proposal process does not currently require cryptographic signatures for proposal authentication.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require proposals to be signed with verified private keys of authorized signers.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Verification reports demonstrating proposal signatures match authorized signers.

checklist_items.title: Secure private keys of Validator/Guardian operators
checklist_items.description: Mandate the use of hardware security modules (HSMs) or multi-signature schemes to protect private keys used for proposal, voting, or administrative actions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator and Guardian private keys are stored in environments susceptible to compromise.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement hardware key management solutions and multi-signature controls.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of key access and signatures, hardware security module deployment details.

checklist_items.title: Implement multisignature approval for administrative contract functions
checklist_items.description: Require multiple independent signatures for critical functions such as contract upgrades, pauses, or mass authorizations to reduce privilege escalation risks.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Administrative functions in Reward Vaults or governance contracts are currently controlled by single private keys or accounts.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy multisig wallets with threshold signatures for admin operations.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig deployment configurations and transaction logs showing multisignature approval.

checklist_items.title: Regular security audits of Reward Vault and Bridge smart contracts
checklist_items.description: Conduct comprehensive audits and formal verification of core smart contracts involved in reward distribution and asset bridging to identify and fix vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vault and Bridge contracts may contain code vulnerabilities exploitable by attackers.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable security firms for audits and audits to include formal verification where applicable.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and verification certificates.

checklist_items.title: Implement access controls and whitelisting for Reward Vault deployment and management
checklist_items.description: Restrict Reward Vault creation, updates, and fund transfers to authorized, properly vetted entities, and maintain a publicly accessible whitelist.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults may be deployed or managed by unvetted entities with potential malicious intent.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy role-based access controls and maintain an on-chain whitelist of trusted entities.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Access control configurations, whitelist records, and deployment logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify contract addresses and deployment sources before user interactions
checklist_items.description: Users should verify that the smart contract addresses and sources they interact with match official verified deployments to prevent spoofing or phishing interactions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users are vulnerable to phishing or misdirection when interacting with unverified contract addresses.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Publish and promote official contract addresses and interfaces; educate users to check these before transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User surveys, documentation of verified addresses, and reports of interactions.

checklist_items.title: Employ anti-phishing and domain verification techniques for user interfaces
checklist_items.description: Implement security measures such as HSTS, content security policies (CSP), and domain validation to prevent spoofed interfaces and protect users from phishing sites.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may be directed to malicious websites or interfaces that resemble legitimate ones.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use HTTPS, strict domain validation, and educate users on verifying website authenticity.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Security configurations, domain validation reports, user reports of phishing.

checklist_items.title: Set explicit transaction verification procedures in user wallets
checklist_items.description: Encourage or enforce users to verify transaction details, amounts, and recipient addresses within their wallets prior to signing, especially for sensitive operations like bridging or staking.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may not verify transaction details before signing, leading to tampering or miscommunication.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Provide clear transaction summaries and verification prompts within wallets.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training programs, wallet interface design documentation.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication mechanisms for RFRV submissions to verify submitter identities.
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Governance Forum requires cryptographic or on-chain verification for users submitting RFRV proposals to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum does not have robust mechanisms to verify the true identity of RFRV submitters.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., digital signatures, verified identities) for all RFRV submitters.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing cryptographic or on-chain verification steps during RFRV submission.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain or cryptographically verifiable attribution for RFRV submissions.
checklist_items.description: Require RFRV submissions to be accompanied by a signed message from an official project address or representative to ensure non-repudiation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The RFRV process lacks strong proof of origin.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use cryptographic signatures from verified project wallets.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signed message transcripts or blockchain signatures linking submissions to verified identities.

checklist_items.title: Secure RPC endpoints used for wallet connections to prevent Man-in-the-Middle attacks.
checklist_items.description: Verify that all wallet connections to RPC endpoints use HTTPS, and that the endpoints are operated by trusted providers with proper certificate management.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RPC communication is vulnerable to MitM attacks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce HTTPS, use trusted RPC providers, and educate users to avoid unverified endpoints.
checklist_items.evidence_required: SSL certificate validation logs, user reports of suspicious RPC URLs.

checklist_items.title: Implement domain verification and user education to prevent phishing of dApp interfaces.
checklist_items.description: Ensure users are aware of official URLs and verify domain ownership, and the system should display clear domain information to users before transaction signing.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may be tricked by phishing sites.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use DNS security measures, educate users, and display domain info prominently.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Educational materials, domain validation logs.

checklist_items.title: Verify whitelist status of Reward Vaults before directing incentives to prevent misallocation.
checklist_items.description: Integrate on-chain verification to ensure that only Reward Vaults in the approved whitelist receive $BGT emissions.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator can direct emissions to any Vault without checks.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement on-chain whitelist checks in the emission process.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing whitelist validation during emission directives.

checklist_items.title: Conduct comprehensive security audits of reward vault smart contracts to prevent vulnerabilities leading to exploits.
checklist_items.description: Perform formal verification, code audits, and bug bounty programs to identify and fix vulnerabilities in Reward Vault contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward Vaults may contain vulnerabilities exploitable by attackers.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Security audits, formal verification, bug bounty initiatives.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, verification documentation, bug bounty submission records.

checklist_items.title: Implement multi-signature and timelock controls for critical administrative functions of Reward Vaults.
checklist_items.description: Add multi-party approvals and timelocked execution of admin actions to prevent privilege escalation or unauthorized changes.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Admin controls may be compromised or misused.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Integrate multisig, enforce timelocks, restrict admin privileges.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction records showing multisig approvals and timelock logs.

checklist_items.title: Ensure private key security for validators and off-chain signers using hardware wallets and operational security practices.
checklist_items.description: Mandate the use of hardware security modules or hardware wallets for signing critical transactions, especially emission directives and validator registration.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Private keys of validators or signers are vulnerable to theft.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce hardware wallet usage, operational security protocols.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management procedure documentation, hardware wallet deployment logs.

checklist_items.title: Regularly audit and test smart contract logic, especially for reward calculation and emission functions.
checklist_items.description: Use formal verification tools and external audits to identify logical flaws in reward distribution and emission code.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward distribution contracts contain complex logic prone to error.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Formal verification, security audits, continuous monitoring.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification approvals.

checklist_items.title: Incorporate rate limiting and anomaly detection for RPC endpoints and dApp interfaces.
checklist_items.description: Implement measures to prevent flood attacks, request spamming, and malicious interaction with RPC and dApp servers.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RPC or dApp servers are vulnerable to overload or abuse.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy rate limiting, CAPTCHAs, and anomaly detection systems.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Server logs, monitoring dashboard alerts.

checklist_items.title: Educate users to verify contract addresses and URLs for all wallet interactions.
checklist_items.description: Provide clear guidelines and warnings for users to avoid phishing, spoofing, and interacting with unverified contracts.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Users may fall victim to phishing or interface spoofing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: User education campaigns, official URL distribution, verification prompts in wallets.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Training materials, user feedback surveys.

checklist_items.title: Use multi-party signatures or consensus mechanisms for critical on-chain governance actions.
checklist_items.description: Require multiple signatures or approval from guardian multisigs before executing sensitive governance proposals or proposal cancellations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Guardians or multisigs may be compromised or misused.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require multiple guardian approvals, implement threshold signatures, audit multisig procedures.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Multisig approval logs, governance decision records.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication for Guardian multisig actions
checklist_items.description: Ensure that the multisig process for proposal cancellation requires multi-factor authentication and secure key storage to prevent spoofing of cancellation signals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: An attacker can compromise at least 5 out of the 9 Guardian private keys.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use multi-signature with hardware security modules, enforce secure key management practices, and perform regular key rotation.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing multisig signature verification and threshold adherence.

checklist_items.title: Secure the Timelock Contract against internal vulnerabilities
checklist_items.description: Conduct thorough security audits and formal verification of the Timelock Contract, especially focusing on proposal cancellation and state mutation functions to prevent tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Timelock Contract might have an exploitable vulnerability allowing unauthorized modification of a proposal's state.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement extensive testing, peer review, formal verification, and bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports, formal verification results, and bug bounty submissions.

checklist_items.title: Verify the implementation of global access controls on Reward Vault contracts
checklist_items.description: Ensure that Reward Vault smart contracts enforce role-based access control (RBAC) and do not permit unauthorized users to invoke administrative functions such as setting incentives or pausing operations.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault contract's access control for functions like adding incentives or setting rates is flawed or bypassable.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement role-based access control with multisig or multisignature governance for privilege escalation functions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Smart contract source code review, access control audit reports.

checklist_items.title: Implement user education and verification practices for dApp interfaces
checklist_items.description: Educate users to verify the domain and contract addresses before signing transactions on dApps, and employ wallet features that display transaction details clearly to prevent spoofing and tampering.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User cannot reliably distinguish a fake version of the dApp interface or contract.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Provide official links, verify contract addresses, promote hardware wallets, and include in-wallet warnings.
checklist_items.evidence_required: User training materials, wallet alerts, verified links on official channels.

checklist_items.title: Deploy smart contracts with formal security audits and bug bounty programs
checklist_items.description: Ensure all reward vaults, bridge contracts, and critical protocol smart contracts undergo formal security audits and are part of bug bounty programs to identify and fix vulnerabilities before deployment.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vaults and bridge contracts might contain exploitable flaws if developed without thorough auditing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Engage reputable security firms, conduct formal verification, and launch bug bounty programs.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit certificates, vulnerability reports, bounty submission logs.

checklist_items.title: Use official domain verification and anti-phishing measures for user interfaces
checklist_items.description: Implement domain verification, SRI (Subresource Integrity), CSP (Content Security Policy), and wallet warnings to prevent users from interacting with spoofed or malicious interfaces.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User does not verify the authenticity of the dApp interface before signing transactions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Deploy official verified domains, implement strict content security policies, and educate users about phishing.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Domain certificates, CSP headers, user awareness protocols.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication linking forum submissions to verified on-chain identities
checklist_items.description: Verify that the Governance Forum requires users to authenticate via their linked on-chain wallet before submitting RFRV proposals.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement cryptographic wallet-based authentication and verified identity linkage.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs showing user login via wallet authentication during RFRV submission.

checklist_items.title: Ensure all governance proposals are validated against a list of approved sources or signatures before execution
checklist_items.description: Verify that each proposal executed via the governance contracts is signed or approved by a trusted authority or multi-signature group.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The governance system does not have a mechanism to verify the origin of proposals.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Require cryptographic signatures from authorized representatives or multisig approval for proposal execution.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Signature verification logs or multisig approval records for proposals.

checklist_items.title: Implement on-chain validation for reward vault whitelist status before accepting emissions
checklist_items.description: Check that any emission instruction to reward vaults is validated on-chain to ensure the vault is whitelisted and active.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator can direct emissions without on-chain verification of vault whitelist status.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Add on-chain whitelist status checks within the emission distribution contract.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Transaction logs showing on-chain verification of vault whitelist status during emissions.

checklist_items.title: Require multi-factor authentication for critical administrative actions on reward vaults
checklist_items.description: Ensure that critical functions like updating incentive rates or whitelisting vaults require multi-factor authentication and are governed by multi-signature or timelock mechanisms.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward vault management functions can be invoked by a single key owner.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce multisig approval and timelock for administrative changes.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit logs of admin actions with multi-signature approval records.

checklist_items.title: Use secure private key management practices for Validator keys signing emission directives
checklist_items.description: Verify that Validator keys used for signing emission or delegation transactions are stored in hardware security modules or multi-signature setups.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Validator private keys can be compromised or mishandled.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement hardware security modules and multi-signature approval workflows for signing critical transactions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management audit reports and hardware security module access logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement comprehensive security audits and formal verification for Reward Vault contracts
checklist_items.description: Conduct thorough audits and formal verification of reward vault smart contracts before deployment to eliminate exploitable vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Reward vault contracts may contain software vulnerabilities.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Contract audits by reputable security firms and formal verification of critical logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and verification certificates.

checklist_items.title: Establish secure, encrypted communication channels for off-chain review discussions
checklist_items.description: Ensure all off-chain communications regarding governance and RFRV reviews are conducted over secure, encrypted channels with strict access controls.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: RFRV reviews may involve sensitive, non-public information.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use end-to-end encrypted messaging platforms and access controls for off-chain discussions.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Communication protocol configurations and access control logs.

checklist_items.title: Implement regular access controls and account security audits for BGT Foundation members
checklist_items.description: Enforce strong authentication, least privilege, and conduct periodic audits of accounts and systems used by BGT Foundation members to prevent privilege escalation and insider threats.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Members may have weak account security leading to privilege escalation.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Enforce MFA, least privilege policies, and conduct regular security audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports on access control and account security.

checklist_items.title: Implement strong authentication linking forum account to on-chain identity
checklist_items.description: Ensure that only verified on-chain identities can submit RFRV proposals to prevent impersonation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Governance Forum lacks strong authentication mechanisms to verify the identity of the user submitting the RFRV.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Implement strong authentication (e.g., linking forum account to on-chain identity, SSO)
checklist_items.evidence_required: Screenshot or audit log showing linked on-chain identity at login.

checklist_items.title: Enforce on-chain whitelist verification for Reward Vaults before emission allocation
checklist_items.description: Check if the smart contracts verify that Reward Vaults are whitelisted and active before directing $BGT emissions to prevent diversion to malicious or unapproved vaults.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: A validator might maliciously or accidentally direct emissions to non-whitelisted or faulty vaults.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Strict on-chain verification that target Reward Vaults are whitelisted and active before emissions are processed.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Logs or transaction records showing emission transactions rejected for non-whitelisted vaults.

checklist_items.title: Conduct security audits and formal verification of Reward Vaults and Validator reward distribution logic
checklist_items.description: Ensure reward distribution mechanisms are resilient against exploits and incorrect calculations, preventing over-rewarding by malicious validators.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: The Reward Vault's incentive mechanisms could have vulnerabilities that attackers exploit for unfair gains.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Rigorous security audits and formal verification of reward logic.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Audit reports and formal verification certificates confirming the absence of reward calculation vulnerabilities.

checklist_items.title: Secure private key management for validators and admin accounts
checklist_items.description: Implement multi-factor authentication, hardware security modules, and operational security procedures for keys controlling emission directions and validator privileges to prevent unauthorized spoofing and privilege escalation.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: Attacker gains access to a Validator's private key to spoof emissions or rewards directions.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Use secure hardware wallets, M-of-N multisig for key management, and conduct regular key audits.
checklist_items.evidence_required: Key management policies and logs demonstrating secure operations.

checklist_items.title: Implement transaction verification and out-of-band confirmation for user-initiated actions
checklist_items.description: Require users to verify transaction details, including token amounts and contract addresses, in their wallet's trusted interface before signing, to prevent tampering by compromised wallets or malicious interfaces.
checklist_items.assumption_ref: User does not meticulously verify transaction details before signing.
checklist_items.mitigation_ref: Encourage use of hardware wallets and display all transaction details explicitly.
